text.NRSV: Then Jehoash son of Jehoahaz took again from Ben-hadad son of Hazael the towns that he had taken from his father Jehoahaz in war. Three times Joash defeated him and recovered the towns of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash son of Jehoahaz again took the cities out of the hand of Ben-hadad son of Hazael, which he had taken out of the hand of Jehoahaz his father, in war,—three times, did Joash smite him, and recover the cities of Israel.
text.KJV: And Jehoash the son of Jehoahaz took again out of the hand of Ben–hadad the son of Hazael the cities, which he had taken out of the hand of Jehoahaz his father by war. Three times did Joash beat him, and recovered the cities of Israel.
text.NABRE: Joash, son of Jehoahaz, took back from Ben-hadad, son of Hazael, the cities Hazael had taken in battle from Jehoahaz, his father. Three times Joash beat him, and thus recovered the cities of Israel.
text.DRB: Moreover Joas the son of Joachaz, took the cities out of the hand of Benadad the son of Hazael, which he had taken out of the hand of Joachaz his father by the right of war, three times did Joas strike him, and he delivered the cities to Israel.

text.NRSV: In the second year of King Joash son of Joahaz of Israel, King Amaziah son of Joash of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the second year of Joash son of Joahaz king of Israel, began Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: In the second year of Joash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel reigned Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah.
text.NABRE: In the second year of Joash, son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel, Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, became king.
text.DRB: In the second year of Joas the son of Joachaz the king of Israel reigned Amasias the son of Joas the king of Juda.

text.NRSV: He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jehoaddin of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-five years old, was he when he began to reign, and twenty-nine years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, his mother’s name, was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: He was twenty and five years old when he began to reign, and reigned twenty and nine years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Jehoaddan of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned twenty-nine years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jehoaddin, from Jerusalem.
text.DRB: Five and twenty years old was he when he began to reign: and nine & twenty years he reigned in Jerusalem, the name of his mother was Ioadan of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, yet not like his ancestor David; in all things he did as his father Joash had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh, only, not like David his father: according to all that Joash his father did, so he did;
text.KJV: And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord, yet not like David his father: he did according to all things as Joash his father did.
text.NABRE: He did what was right in the Lord ’s eyes, though not like David his father. He did just as his father Joash had done,
text.DRB: And he did right before our Lord, but yet not as David his father. He did according to all things, which Joas his father did:

text.NRSV: But the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.
text.Rotherham: only, the high places, took they not away,—still were the people sacrificing and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: Howbeit the high places were not taken away: as yet the people did sacrifice and burnt incense on the high places.
text.NABRE: though the high places did not disappear, and the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places.
text.DRB: but this only that he took not away the excelses: for yet the people immolated, and burnt incense in the excelses.

text.NRSV: As soon as the royal power was firmly in his hand, he killed his servants who had murdered his father the king.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that, as soon as the kingdom was confirmed in his hand, he smote his servants who smote the king his father;
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as soon as the kingdom was confirmed in his hand, that he slew his servants which had slain the king his father.
text.NABRE: When Amaziah had the kingdom firmly in hand, he struck down the officials who had struck down the king, his father.
text.DRB: And when he obtained the Kingdom, he smote his servants, which had slain the king his father:

text.NRSV: But he did not put to death the children of the murderers; according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses, where the Lord commanded, “Parents shall not be put to death for the children, nor shall children be put to death for the parents; but all shall be put to death for their own sins.”
text.Rotherham: but, the sons of them that smote him, slew he not,—as it is written in the book of the law of Moses, which Yahweh commanded, saying—Fathers, shall not be put to death, for sons, nor shall, sons, be put to death, for fathers, but every man, for his own sin, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: But the children of the murderers he slew not: according unto that which is written in the book of the law of Moses, wherein the Lord commanded, saying, The fathers shall not be put to death for the children, nor the children be put to death for the fathers; but every man shall be put to death for his own sin.
text.NABRE: But their children he did not put to death, according to what is written in the book of the law of Moses, which the Lord commanded: “Parents shall not be put to death for their children, nor shall children be put to death for their parents; only for one’s own crimes shall a person be put to death.”
text.DRB: but their children that killed him, he did not put to death, according to that which is written in the book of the law of Moyses, as our Lord commanded, saying: The fathers shall not die for the children, neither shall the children die for the fathers: but every one shall die in his own sin.

text.NRSV: He killed ten thousand Edomites in the Valley of Salt and took Sela by storm; he called it Joktheel, which is its name to this day.
text.Rotherham: He, smote Edom, in the valley of salt, ten thousand, and seized Sela, in the war,—and called the name thereof Joktheel, [as it is] until this day.
text.KJV: He slew of Edom in the valley of salt ten thousand, and took Selah by war, and called the name of it Joktheel unto this day.
text.NABRE: Amaziah struck down ten thousand Edomites in the Salt Valley. He took Sela in battle and renamed it Joktheel, the name it has to this day.
text.DRB: He smote Edom in the Vale of Salt pits ten thousand, and took the rock in battle, & called the name thereof, Jectehel, until this present day.

text.NRSV: Then Amaziah sent messengers to King Jehoash son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu of Israel, saying, “Come, let us look one another in the face.”
text.Rotherham: Then, sent Amaziah messengers unto Jehoash son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying,—Come now, let us look one another in the face.
text.KJV: Then Amaziah sent messengers to Jehoash, the son of Jehoahaz son of Jehu, king of Israel, saying, Come, let us look one another in the face.
text.NABRE: Then Amaziah sent messengers to Joash, son of Jehoahaz, son of Jehu, king of Israel, with this message: “Come, let us meet face to face.”
text.DRB: Then Amasias sent messengers to Joas the son of Joachaz, the son of Jehu the king of Israel, saying: Come let us see one another.

text.NRSV: King Jehoash of Israel sent word to King Amaziah of Judah, “A thornbush on Lebanon sent to a cedar on Lebanon, saying, ‘Give your daughter to my son for a wife’; but a wild animal of Lebanon passed by and trampled down the thornbush.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash king of Israel sent unto Amaziah king of Judah, saying, A thistle that was in Lebanon, sent unto a cedar that was in Lebanon, saying—Give thy daughter to my son to wife,—and there passed by a beast of the field that was in Lebanon, and trampled down the thistle:
text.KJV: And Jehoash the king of Israel sent to Amaziah king of Judah, saying, The thistle that was in Lebanon sent to the cedar that was in Lebanon, saying, Give thy daughter to my son to wife: and there passed by a wild beast that was in Lebanon, and trode down the thistle.
text.NABRE: Joash, king of Israel, sent this reply to Amaziah, king of Judah: “A thistle of Lebanon sent word to a cedar of Lebanon, ‘Give your daughter to my son in marriage,’ but an animal of Lebanon passed by and trampled the thistle underfoot.
text.DRB: And Joas the king of Israel sent again to Amasias the king of Juda saying: A thistle of Libanus sent to a cedar tree, which is in Libanus, saying: Give thy daughter to my son to wife. And the beasts of the forest, that are in Libanus, passed & trod the thistle.

text.NRSV: You have indeed defeated Edom, and your heart has lifted you up. Be content with your glory, and stay at home; for why should you provoke trouble so that you fall, you and Judah with you?”
text.Rotherham: Thou hast, smitten, Edom, and, thy heart, would lift thee up,—Glory, and stay at home! Wherefore, then, shouldest thou contend with misfortune, and fall, thou, and Judah with thee?
text.KJV: Thou hast indeed smitten Edom, and thine heart hath lifted thee up: glory of this, and tarry at home: for why shouldest thou meddle to thy hurt, that thou shouldest fall, even thou, and Judah with thee?
text.NABRE: You have indeed struck down Edom, and your heart is lifted up; enjoy your glory, but stay home! Why bring misfortune and failure on yourself and on Judah with you?”
text.DRB: Thou striking hast prevailed over Edom, and thy heart hath puffed thee up: be content with the glory, and sit in thy house: Why provokest thou evil, that thou mayst fall & Juda with thee?

text.NRSV: But Amaziah would not listen. So King Jehoash of Israel went up; he and King Amaziah of Judah faced one another in battle at Beth-shemesh, which belongs to Judah.
text.Rotherham: But Amaziah hearkened not. So then Jehoash king of Israel came up, and they looked one another in the face, he and Amaziah king of Judah,—in Beth-shemesh, which belongeth unto Judah.
text.KJV: But Amaziah would not hear. Therefore Jehoash king of Israel went up; and he and Amaziah king of Judah looked one another in the face at Beth–shemesh, which belongeth to Judah.
text.NABRE: But Amaziah did not listen. So Joash, king of Israel, advanced, and he and Amaziah, king of Judah, met face to face at Beth-shemesh of Judah,
text.DRB: And Amasias agreed not. And Joas the king of Israel went up, and they saw each other, he & Amasias the king of Juda in Bethsames a town of Juda.

text.NRSV: Judah was defeated by Israel; everyone fled home.
text.Rotherham: Then was Judah defeated before Israel,—and they fled, every man to his own home;
text.KJV: And Judah was put to the worse before Israel; and they fled every man to their tents.
text.NABRE: and Judah was defeated by Israel, and all fled to their tents.
text.DRB: And Juda was strucken before Israel, & every man fled into their tabernacles.

text.NRSV: King Jehoash of Israel captured King Amaziah of Judah son of Jehoash son of Ahaziah at Beth-shemesh; he came to Jerusalem, and broke down the wall of Jerusalem from the Ephraim Gate to the Corner Gate, a distance of four hundred cubits.
text.Rotherham: and, upon Amaziah, king of Judah, son of Jehoash son of Ahaziah, did Jehoash king of Israel, seize, in Beth-shemesh,—and entered Jerusalem, and brake down the wall of Jerusalem, at the gate of Ephraim, as far as the corner gate, four hundred cubits;
text.KJV: And Jehoash king of Israel took Amaziah king of Judah, the son of Jehoash the son of Ahaziah, at Beth–shemesh, and came to Jerusalem, and brake down the wall of Jerusalem from the gate of Ephraim unto the corner gate, four hundred cubits.
text.NABRE: But Amaziah, king of Judah, son of Joash, son of Ahaziah, was captured by Joash, king of Israel, at Beth-shemesh. When they came to Jerusalem Joash tore down the wall of Jerusalem, from the Gate of Ephraim to the Corner Gate, four hundred cubits.
text.DRB: But Joas the king of Israel did take Amasias the king of Juda the son of Joas, the son of Ochozias, in Bethsames, & brought him into Jerusalem: and he brake down the wall of Jerusalem, from the gate of Ephraim unto the gate of the corner, four hundred cubits.

text.NRSV: He seized all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king’s house, as well as hostages; then he returned to Samaria.
text.Rotherham: and took all the gold and the silver and all the vessels that were found in the house of Yahweh, and in the treasuries of the house of the king, and hostages,—and returned to Samaria.
text.KJV: And he took all the gold and silver, and all the vessels that were found in the house of the Lord, and in the treasures of the king’s house, and hostages, and returned to Samaria.
text.NABRE: He took all the gold and silver and all the vessels found in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king’s house, and hostages as well. Then he returned to Samaria.
text.DRB: And he took all the gold, and silver, and all the vessels, that were found in the house of our Lord, and in the king's treasures, and hostages, and returned into Samaria.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts that Jehoash did, his might, and how he fought with King Amaziah of Judah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jehoash, what he did, and his might and how he fought with Amaziah king of Judah, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Jehoash which he did, and his might, and how he fought with Amaziah king of Judah, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Joash, what he did and his valor, and how he made war against Amaziah, king of Judah, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Joas which he did, and his strength, wherewith he fought against Amasias the king of Juda, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: Jehoash slept with his ancestors, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel; then his son Jeroboam succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jehoash slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria, with the kings of Israel,—and, Jeroboam his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Jehoash slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel; and Jeroboam his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Joash rested with his ancestors; he was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel, and his son Jeroboam succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Joas slept with his fathers, and was buried in Samaria with the kings of Israel: and Jeroboam his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: King Amaziah son of Joash of Judah lived fifteen years after the death of King Jehoash son of Jehoahaz of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Amaziah son of Joash king of Judah, lived, after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel, fifteen years.
text.KJV: And Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah lived after the death of Jehoash son of Jehoahaz king of Israel fifteen years.
text.NABRE: Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, survived Joash, son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel, by fifteen years.
text.DRB: And Amasias the son of Joas the king of Juda lived, after that Joas the son of Joachaz the king of Israel was dead, fifteen years.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Amaziah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Amaziah, is, it, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Amaziah, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Amaziah are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Amasias, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: A conspiracy was formed against him in Jerusalem, and he fled to Lachish. But they sent after him to Lachish, and killed him there.
text.Rotherham: And, when they made against him a conspiracy in Jerusalem, he fled to Lachish,—but they sent after him to Lachish, and slew him, there.
text.KJV: Now they made a conspiracy against him in Jerusalem: and he fled to Lachish; but they sent after him to Lachish, and slew him there.
text.NABRE: When a conspiracy was formed against him in Jerusalem, he fled to Lachish. But he was pursued to Lachish and killed there.
text.DRB: And there was a conspiracy made against him in Jerusalem: But he fled into Lachis. And they sent after him into Lachis, and killed him there.

text.NRSV: They brought him on horses; he was buried in Jerusalem with his ancestors in the city of David.
text.Rotherham: And they bare him on horses,—and he was buried in Jerusalem, with his fathers, in the city of David.
text.KJV: And they brought him on horses: and he was buried at Jerusalem with his fathers in the city of David.
text.NABRE: He was brought back on horses and was buried in Jerusalem with his ancestors in the City of David.
text.DRB: And they carried him away upon horses, and he was buried in Jerusalem with his fathers in the City of David.

text.NRSV: All the people of Judah took Azariah, who was sixteen years old, and made him king to succeed his father Amaziah.
text.Rotherham: And all the people of Judah took Azariah, he, being sixteen yearn old,—and made, him, king instead of his father Amaziah.
text.KJV: And all the people of Judah took Azariah, which was sixteen years old, and made him king instead of his father Amaziah.
text.NABRE: Thereupon all the people of Judah took Azariah, who was only sixteen years old, and made him king to succeed Amaziah, his father.
text.DRB: And all the people of Juda took Azarias sixteen years old, and made him king for his father Amasias.

text.NRSV: He rebuilt Elath and restored it to Judah, after the king slept with his ancestors.
text.Rotherham: He, built Elath, and restored it to Judah,—after the king slept with his fathers.
text.KJV: He built Elath, and restored it to Judah, after that the king slept with his fathers.
text.NABRE: It was he who rebuilt Elath and restored it to Judah, after the king rested with his ancestors.
text.DRB: He built Ælath, and restored it to Juda, after that the king slept with his fathers.

text.NRSV: In the fifteenth year of King Amaziah son of Joash of Judah, King Jeroboam son of Joash of Israel began to reign in Samaria; he reigned forty-one years.
text.Rotherham: In the fifteenth year of Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, began Jeroboam, son of Joash, king of Israel, to reign in Samaria, [and he reigned] forty-one years.
text.KJV: In the fifteenth year of Amaziah the son of Joash king of Judah Jeroboam the son of Joash king of Israel began to reign in Samaria, and reigned forty and one years.
text.NABRE: Reign of Jeroboam II of Israel. In the fifteenth year of Amaziah, son of Joash, king of Judah, Jeroboam, son of Joash, king of Israel, became king in Samaria for forty-one years.
text.DRB: In the fifteenth year of Amasias the son of Joas the king of Juda, reigned Jeroboam the son of Joas the king of Israel in Samaria, one and forty years:

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from all the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from any of the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord: he departed not from all the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.
text.NABRE: He did evil in the Lord ’s sight; he did not desist from any of the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.
text.DRB: and he did that which is evil before our Lord. He departed not from all the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.

text.NRSV: He restored the border of Israel from Lebo-hamath as far as the Sea of the Arabah, according to the word of the Lord, the God of Israel, which he spoke by his servant Jonah son of Amittai, the prophet, who was from Gath-hepher.
text.Rotherham: He, restored the boundary of Israel, from the entering in of Hamath, unto the sea of the waste plain,—according to the word of Yahweh, God of Israel, which he spake by the hand of his servant Jonah, son of Amittai, the prophet, who was of Gath-hepher.
text.KJV: He restored the coast of Israel from the entering of Hamath unto the sea of the plain, according to the word of the Lord God of Israel, which he spake by the hand of his servant Jonah, the son of Amittai, the prophet, which was of Gath–hepher.
text.NABRE: He restored the boundaries of Israel from Lebo-hamath to the sea of the Arabah, as the Lord, the God of Israel, had foretold through his servant, the prophet Jonah, son of Amittai, from Gath-hepher.
text.DRB: He restored the borders of Israel from the entrance of Emath, unto the Sea of the wilderness, according to the word of our Lord the God of Israel, which he spake by his servant Jonas the son of Amathi, the Prophet, who was of Geth, which is in Opher.

text.NRSV: For the Lord saw that the distress of Israel was very bitter; there was no one left, bond or free, and no one to help Israel.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh saw the humiliation of Israel, that it was bitter, indeed,—and that there was no one shut up, nor any one left at large, no one indeed to help Israel.
text.KJV: For the Lord saw the affliction of Israel, that it was very bitter: for there was not any shut up, nor any left, nor any helper for Israel.
text.NABRE: For the Lord saw the very bitter affliction of Israel, where there was neither bond nor free, no one at all to help Israel.
text.DRB: For our Lord saw the affliction of Israel exceeding bitter, & that they were consumed unto the imprisoned & meanest persons, and that there was none to help Israel.

text.NRSV: But the Lord had not said that he would blot out the name of Israel from under heaven, so he saved them by the hand of Jeroboam son of Joash.
text.Rotherham: Neither had Yahweh spoken, to wipe out the name of Israel from under the heavens,—so he saved them, by the hand of Jeroboam son of Joash.
text.KJV: And the Lord said not that he would blot out the name of Israel from under heaven: but he saved them by the hand of Jeroboam the son of Joash.
text.NABRE: Since the Lord had not resolved to wipe out the name of Israel from under the heavens, he saved them through Jeroboam, son of Joash.
text.DRB: Neither did our Lord determine that he would destroy the name of Israel from under heaven, but he saved them in the hand of Jeroboam the son of Joas.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might, how he fought, and how he recovered for Israel Damascus and Hamath, which had belonged to Judah, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might when he warred, and how he restored Damascus and Hamath to Judah in Israel, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his might, how he warred, and how he recovered Damascus, and Hamath, which belonged to Judah, for Israel, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Jeroboam, with all that he did and his valor, how he fought, and how he regained Damascus and Hamath for Israel, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Jeroboam, and all that he did, and his strength, wherewith he fought, and how he restored Damascus, and Emath to Juda in Israel, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: Jeroboam slept with his ancestors, the kings of Israel; and his son Zechariah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jeroboam slept with his fathers, with the kings of Israel,—and, Zechariah his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Jeroboam slept with his fathers, even with the kings of Israel; and Zachariah his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Jeroboam rested with his ancestors, the kings of Israel, and his son Zechariah succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Jeroboam slept with his fathers the kings of Israel, and Zacharias his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the twenty-seventh year of King Jeroboam of Israel, Azariah son of King Amaziah of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam king of Israel, began Azariah son of Amaziah king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: In the twenty and seventh year of Jeroboam king of Israel began Azariah son of Amaziah king of Judah to reign.
text.NABRE: In the twenty-seventh year of Jeroboam, king of Israel, Azariah, son of Amaziah, king of Judah, became king.
text.DRB: In the seven and twentieth year of Jeroboam the king of Israel reigned Azarias the son of Amasias the king of Juda.

text.NRSV: He was sixteen years old when he began to reign, and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jecoliah of Jerusalem.
text.Rotherham: Sixteen years old, was he when he began to reign, and, fifty-two years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Jecholiah, of Jerusalem.
text.KJV: Sixteen years old was he when he began to reign, and he reigned two and fifty years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Jecholiah of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: He was sixteen years old when he became king, and he reigned fifty-two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jecholiah, from Jerusalem.
text.DRB: He was sixteen years old, when he began to reign, and two and fifty years he reigned in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Jechelia of Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, just as his father Amaziah had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh,—according to all that, Amaziah his father, had done.
text.KJV: And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord, according to all that his father Amaziah had done;
text.NABRE: He did what was right in the Lord ’s sight, just as his father Amaziah had done,
text.DRB: And he did that which was liked before our Lord, according to all things that his father Amasias did

text.NRSV: Nevertheless the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places.
text.Rotherham: Only, the high places, took they not away,—still were the people sacrificing and burning incense in the high places.
text.KJV: Save that the high places were not removed: the people sacrificed and burnt incense still on the high places.
text.NABRE: though the high places did not disappear, and the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places.
text.DRB: But the excelses he destroyed not: as yet the people sacrificed, & burnt incense in the excelses.

text.NRSV: The Lord struck the king, so that he was leprous to the day of his death, and lived in a separate house. Jotham the king’s son was in charge of the palace, governing the people of the land.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh smote the king, so that he became a leper, until the day of his death, and dwelt in a lazar-house,—and, Jotham, son of the king, was over the house, judging the people of the land.
text.KJV: And the Lord smote the king, so that he was a leper unto the day of his death, and dwelt in a several house. And Jotham the king’s son was over the house, judging the people of the land.
text.NABRE: The Lord afflicted the king, and he was a leper until the day he died. He lived in a house apart, while Jotham, the king’s son, was master of the palace and ruled the people of the land.
text.DRB: And our Lord struck the king, and he was a leper until the day of his death, & he dwelt in a free house a-part: but Joathan the king's son governed the palace, and judged the people of the land.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Azariah, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Azariah, and all that he did, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah.
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Azariah, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Azariah, and all that he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Azarias, and all that he did, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: Azariah slept with his ancestors, and they buried him with his ancestors in the city of David; his son Jotham succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Azariah slept with his fathers, and they buried him with his fathers, in the city of David,—and, Jotham his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: So Azariah slept with his fathers; and they buried him with his fathers in the city of David: and Jotham his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Azariah rested with his ancestors, and was buried with them in the City of David, and his son Jotham succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Azarias slept with his fathers: and they buried him with his ancestors in the city of David, & Joathan his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-eighth year of King Azariah of Judah, Zechariah son of Jeroboam reigned over Israel in Samaria six months.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-eighth year of Azariah king of Judah, began Zechariah son of Jeroboam to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] six months.
text.KJV: In the thirty and eighth year of Azariah king of Judah did Zachariah the son of Jeroboam reign over Israel in Samaria six months.
text.NABRE: Reign of Zechariah of Israel. In the thirty-eighth year of Azariah, king of Judah, Zechariah, son of Jeroboam, became king over Israel in Samaria for six months.
text.DRB: In the eight and thirtieth year of Azarias the king of Juda, reigned Zacharias the son of Jeroboam over Israel in Samaria six months:

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, as his ancestors had done. He did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh, as did his fathers,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord, as his fathers had done: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, as his ancestors had done, and did not desist from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.
text.DRB: and he did that which is evil before our Lord, as his fathers had done: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Jabesh conspired against him, and struck him down in public and killed him, and succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: Then conspired against him, Shallum son of Jabesh, and smote him before the people, and slew him,—and reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: And Shallum the son of Jabesh conspired against him, and smote him before the people, and slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Shallum, son of Jabesh, plotted against him and struck him down at Ibleam. He killed him and reigned in his place.
text.DRB: And Sellum the son of Jabes conspired against him: and smote him openly, killed him, and reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Zechariah are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Zechariah, behold it! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel.
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Zachariah, behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: As for the rest of the acts of Zechariah, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: but the rest of the words of Zacharias, are not these written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: This was the promise of the Lord that he gave to Jehu, “Your sons shall sit on the throne of Israel to the fourth generation.” And so it happened.
text.Rotherham: That, was the word of Yahweh, which he spake unto Jehu, saying, Thy sons, to four generations, shall sit on the throne of Israel. And it was so!
text.KJV: This was the word of the Lord which he spake unto Jehu, saying, Thy sons shall sit on the throne of Israel unto the fourth generation. And so it came to pass.
text.NABRE: This was the word the Lord had spoken to Jehu: Sons of your line to the fourth generation shall sit upon the throne of Israel; and so it was.
text.DRB: This is the word of our Lord, which he spake to Jehu, saying: Thy children shall sit unto the fourth generation upon the throne of Israel. And so it came to pass.

text.NRSV: Shallum son of Jabesh began to reign in the thirty-ninth year of King Uzziah of Judah; he reigned one month in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: Shallum the son of Jabesh, began to reign, in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah, king of Judah,—for the space of a month in Samaria.
text.KJV: Shallum the son of Jabesh began to reign in the nine and thirtieth year of Uzziah king of Judah; and he reigned a full month in Samaria.
text.NABRE: Reign of Shallum of Israel. Shallum, son of Jabesh, became king in the thirty-ninth year of Uzziah, king of Judah; he reigned one month in Samaria.
text.DRB: Sellum the son of Jabes reigned the nine and thirtieth year of Azarias the king of Juda: and reigned one month in Samaria.

text.NRSV: Then Menahem son of Gadi came up from Tirzah and came to Samaria; he struck down Shallum son of Jabesh in Samaria and killed him, and succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: Then came up Menahem son of Gadi, from Tirzah, and entered Samaria, and smote Shallum son of Jabesh in Samaria,—and slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: For Menahem the son of Gadi went up from Tirzah, and came to Samaria, and smote Shallum the son of Jabesh in Samaria, and slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Menahem, son of Gadi, came up from Tirzah to Samaria, and struck down Shallum, son of Jabesh, in Samaria. He killed him and reigned in his place.
text.DRB: And Manahem the son of Gadi went up from Thersa: and he came into Samaria, and smote Sellum the son of Jabes in Samaria, and slew him, and reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Shallum, including the conspiracy that he made, are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Shallum, and his conspiracy which he made, behold them! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel!
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Shallum, and his conspiracy which he made, behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: As for the rest of the acts of Shallum, with the conspiracy he carried out, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Sellum, and his conspiracy, wherewith he wrought treason, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: At that time Menahem sacked Tiphsah, all who were in it and its territory from Tirzah on; because they did not open it to him, he sacked it. He even ripped up all the pregnant women in it.
text.Rotherham: Then, Menahem smote Tiphsah, and all that were therein, and the boundaries thereof, from Tirzah, because they opened not, therefore he smote them,—all the women therein that were with child, he ripped up.
text.KJV: Then Menahem smote Tiphsah, and all that were therein, and the coasts thereof from Tirzah: because they opened not to him, therefore he smote it; and all the women therein that were with child he ripped up.
text.NABRE: At that time, Menahem attacked Tappuah, all its inhabitants, and its whole district as far as Tirzah, because they did not let him in. He attacked them; he even ripped open all their pregnant women.
text.DRB: Then Manahem struck Thapsa and all that were in it and the borders thereof from Thersa, for they would not open unto him: and he slew all women thereof that were with child, and ripped them.

text.NRSV: In the thirty-ninth year of King Azariah of Judah, Menahem son of Gadi began to reign over Israel; he reigned ten years in Samaria.
text.Rotherham: In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah king of Judah, began Menahem son of Gadi to reign over Israel, ten years [reigned he], in Samaria.
text.KJV: In the nine and thirtieth year of Azariah king of Judah began Menahem the son of Gadi to reign over Israel, and reigned ten years in Samaria.
text.NABRE: Reign of Menahem of Israel. In the thirty-ninth year of Azariah, king of Judah, Menahem, son of Gadi, became king over Israel for ten years in Samaria.
text.DRB: In the nine and thirtieth year of Azarias the king of Juda, reigned Manahem the son of Gadi over Israel ten years in Samaria.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart all his days from any of the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit, all his days.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord: he departed not all his days from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight as long as he lived, not desisting from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.
text.DRB: And he did that which was evil before our Lord: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin all his days.

text.NRSV: King Pul of Assyria came against the land; Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver, so that he might help him confirm his hold on the royal power.
text.Rotherham: Pul the king of Assyria came against the land, so Menahem gave unto Pul, a thousand talents of silver,—that his hands might be with him, to confirm the kingdom, in his hand.
text.KJV: And Pul the king of Assyria came against the land: and Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver, that his hand might be with him to confirm the kingdom in his hand.
text.NABRE: Pul, king of Assyria, came against the land. But Menahem gave Pul a thousand talents of silver to have his help in holding onto his kingdom.
text.DRB: Phul also the king of the Assyrians came into the land, and Manahem gave to Phul a thousand talents of silver, that he should aid him, and establish his Kingdom.

text.NRSV: Menahem exacted the money from Israel, that is, from all the wealthy, fifty shekels of silver from each one, to give to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria turned back, and did not stay there in the land.
text.Rotherham: And Menahem exacted the silver of Israel, of all the mighty men of wealth, to give to the king of Assyria, fifty shekels of silver, of each man,—so the king of Assyria turned back, and stayed not there, in the land.
text.KJV: And Menahem exacted the money of Israel, even of all the mighty men of wealth, of each man fifty shekels of silver, to give to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria turned back, and stayed not there in the land.
text.NABRE: Menahem paid out silver on behalf of Israel, that is, for all the people of substance, by giving the king of Assyria fifty shekels of silver for each one. So the king of Assyria went home and did not stay in the land.
text.DRB: And Manahem put a tax of silver upon Israel, on them that were mighty and rich to give the king of the Assyrians, fifty sicles of silver every man: and the king of the Assyrians returned, and tarried not in the land.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Menahem, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Menahem, and all that he did, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel?
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Menahem, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Menahem, with all that he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Manahem, and all that he did, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: Menahem slept with his ancestors; then his son Pekahiah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Menahem slept with his fathers,—and, Pekahiah his son, reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: And Menahem slept with his fathers; and Pekahiah his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Menahem rested with his ancestors, and his son Pekahiah succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Manahem slept with his fathers: and Phaceia his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the fiftieth year of King Azariah of Judah, Pekahiah son of Menahem began to reign over Israel in Samaria; he reigned two years.
text.Rotherham: In the fiftieth year of Azariah king of Judah, began Pekahiah son of Menahem to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] two years.
text.KJV: In the fiftieth year of Azariah king of Judah Pekahiah the son of Menahem began to reign over Israel in Samaria, and reigned two years.
text.NABRE: Reign of Pekahiah of Israel. In the fiftieth year of Azariah, king of Judah, Pekahiah, son of Menahem, became king over Israel in Samaria for two years.
text.DRB: In the fiftieth year of Azarias the king of Juda reigned Phaceia the son of Manahem over Israel in Samaria two years:

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, not desisting from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.
text.DRB: and he did that which was evil before our Lord: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.

text.NRSV: Pekah son of Remaliah, his captain, conspired against him with fifty of the Gileadites, and struck him down in Samaria, in the citadel of the palace besides Argob and Arieh; he killed him and succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And there conspired against him—Pekah son of Remaliah, a hero of his, and smote him in Samaria, in the citadel of the house of a king, with Argob and with Arieh, and, with him, fifty men of the sons of the Gileadites,—and he slew him, and reigned in his stead.
text.KJV: But Pekah the son of Remaliah, a captain of his, conspired against him, and smote him in Samaria, in the palace of the king’s house, with Argob and Arieh, and with him fifty men of the Gileadites: and he killed him, and reigned in his room.
text.NABRE: His adjutant Pekah, son of Remaliah, conspired against him, and struck him down at Samaria within the palace stronghold; he had with him fifty men from Gilead. He killed him and reigned in his place.
text.DRB: And Phacee the son of Romelia, his captain conspired against him, and struck him in Samaria, in the tower of the king's house beside Argob and beside Arie, and with him fifty men of the children of the Galaadites, and he slew him, and reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Pekahiah, and all that he did, are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Pekahiah, and all that he did, behold them! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel!
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Pekahiah, and all that he did, behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: As for the rest of the acts of Pekahiah, with all that he did, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Phaceia and all that he did, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel.

text.NRSV: In the fifty-second year of King Azariah of Judah, Pekah son of Remaliah began to reign over Israel in Samaria; he reigned twenty years.
text.Rotherham: In the fifty-second year of Azariah king of Judah, began Pekah son of Remaliah to reign over Israel, in Samaria, [and he reigned] twenty years.
text.KJV: In the two and fiftieth year of Azariah king of Judah Pekah the son of Remaliah began to reign over Israel in Samaria, and reigned twenty years.
text.NABRE: Reign of Pekah of Israel. In the fifty-second year of Azariah, king of Judah, Pekah, son of Remaliah, became king over Israel in Samaria for twenty years.
text.DRB: In the two and fiftieth year of Azarias the king of Juda reigned Phacee the son of Romelia over Israel in Samaria twenty years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord; he did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused Israel to commit.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—he turned not away from the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, which he caused, Israel, to commit.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nebat, who made Israel to sin.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, not desisting from the sins that Jeroboam, son of Nebat, had caused Israel to commit.
text.DRB: And he did that which was evil before our Lord: he departed not from the sins of Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who made Israel to sin.

text.NRSV: In the days of King Pekah of Israel, King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria came and captured Ijon, Abel-beth-maacah, Janoah, Kedesh, Hazor, Gilead, and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali; and he carried the people captive to Assyria.
text.Rotherham: In the days of Pekah king of Israel, came Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, and took Ijon, and Abel-beth-maachah, and Janoah, and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Gilead, and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali—and carried them away captive to Assyria.
text.KJV: In the days of Pekah king of Israel came Tiglath–pileser king of Assyria, and took Ijon, and Abel–beth–maachah, and Janoah, and Kedesh, and Hazor, and Gilead, and Galilee, all the land of Naphtali, and carried them captive to Assyria.
text.NABRE: In the days of Pekah, king of Israel, Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, came and took Ijon, Abel-beth-maacah, Janoah, Kedesh, Hazor, Gilead, and Galilee—all the land of Naphtali—deporting the inhabitants to Assyria.
text.DRB: In the days of Phacee the king of Israel came Theglathphalasar the king of Assur, and took Aion, and Abel, the house of Maachat & Janoe, and Cedes, and Asor, and Galaad, and Galilee, and all the land of Nephthali: and transported them unto the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: Then Hoshea son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekah son of Remaliah, and struck him down, and killed him, and succeeded him, in the twentieth year of Jotham son of Uzziah.
text.Rotherham: And Hoshea son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekah, son of Remaliah, and smote him, and slew him, and reigned in his stead,—in the twentieth year of Jotham, son of Uzziah.
text.KJV: And Hoshea the son of Elah made a conspiracy against Pekah the son of Remaliah, and smote him, and slew him, and reigned in his stead, in the twentieth year of Jotham the son of Uzziah.
text.NABRE: Hoshea, son of Elah, carried out a conspiracy against Pekah, son of Remaliah; he struck and killed him, and succeeded him as king in the twentieth year of Jotham, son of Uzziah.
text.DRB: And Osee the son of Ela conspired, and lay in wait against Phacee the son of Romelia, and struck him, and slew him: and he reigned for him the twentieth year of Joatham the son of Ozias

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Pekah, and all that he did, are written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Pekah, and all that he did, behold them! written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Israel!
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Pekah, and all that he did, behold, they are written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.NABRE: As for the rest of the acts of Pekah, with all that he did, these are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Israel.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Phacee, and all that he did, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Israel?

text.NRSV: In the second year of King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel, King Jotham son of Uzziah of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the second year of Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel, began Jotham son of Uzziah king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: In the second year of Pekah the son of Remaliah king of Israel began Jotham the son of Uzziah king of Judah to reign.
text.NABRE: Reign of Jotham of Judah. In the second year of Pekah, son of Remaliah, king of Israel, Jotham, son of Uzziah, king of Judah, became king.
text.DRB: In the second year of Phacee the son of Romelia the king of Israel, reigned Joatham the son of Ozias the king of Juda.

text.NRSV: He was twenty-five years old when he began to reign and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jerusha daughter of Zadok.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-five years old, was he, when he began to reign, and, sixteen years, reigned he, in Jerusalem,—and, the name of his mother, was Jerusha, daughter of Zadok.
text.KJV: Five and twenty years old was he when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Jerusha, the daughter of Zadok.
text.NABRE: He was twenty-five years old when he became king, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jerusha, daughter of Zadok.
text.DRB: Five and twenty years old was he when he began to reign, and sixteen years he reigned in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Jerusa, the daughter of Sadoc.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, just as his father Uzziah had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh,—according to all that Uzziah his father had done.
text.KJV: And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord: he did according to all that his father Uzziah had done.
text.NABRE: He did what was right in the Lord ’s sight, just as his father Uzziah had done,
text.DRB: And he did that which was liked before our Lord: according to all things, which Ozias his father had done, did he work.

text.NRSV: Nevertheless the high places were not removed; the people still sacrificed and made offerings on the high places. He built the upper gate of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Only, the high places, took they not away, still were the people offering sacrifice and burning incense in the high places,—he, built the upper gate of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Howbeit the high places were not removed: the people sacrificed and burned incense still in the high places. He built the higher gate of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: though the high places did not disappear, and the people continued to sacrifice and to burn incense on the high places. It was he who built the Upper Gate of the Lord ’s house.
text.DRB: Howbeit the excelses he took not away: as yet the people immolated, and burnt incense in the excelses: he built the highest gate of the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Jotham, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Jotham, what he did, is, it, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Jotham, and all that he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Jotham, with what he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Joatham, and all that he did, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: In those days the Lord began to send King Rezin of Aram and Pekah son of Remaliah against Judah.
text.Rotherham: In those days, began Yahweh to send against Judah, Rezin, king of Syria,—and Pekah son of Remaliah.
text.KJV: In those days the Lord began to send against Judah Rezin the king of Syria, and Pekah the son of Remaliah.
text.NABRE: It was at that time that the Lord began to unleash Rezin, king of Aram, and Pekah, son of Remaliah, against Judah.
text.DRB: In those days our Lord began to send into Juda Rasin the king of Syria, and Phacee the son of Romelia.

text.NRSV: Jotham slept with his ancestors, and was buried with his ancestors in the city of David; his son Ahaz succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Jotham slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers, in the city of David his father,—and, Ahaz his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Jotham slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David his father: and Ahaz his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Jotham rested with his ancestors; he was buried with his ancestors in the City of David his father, and his son Ahaz succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Joatham slept with his fathers, and was buried with them in the city of David his father, and Achaz his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, King Ahaz son of Jotham of Judah began to reign.
text.Rotherham: In the seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah, began Ahaz son of Jotham king of Judah to reign.
text.KJV: In the seventeenth year of Pekah the son of Remaliah Ahaz the son of Jotham king of Judah began to reign.
text.NABRE: In the seventeenth year of Pekah, son of Remaliah, Ahaz, son of Jotham, king of Judah, became king.
text.DRB: In the seventeenth year of Phacee the son of Romelia reigned Achaz the son of Joatham king of Juda.

text.NRSV: Ahaz was twenty years old when he began to reign; he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. He did not do what was right in the sight of the Lord his God, as his ancestor David had done,
text.Rotherham: Twenty years old, was Ahaz when he began to reign, and, sixteen years, reigned he in Jerusalem. And he did not that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh his God, like David his father;
text.KJV: Twenty years old was Ahaz when he began to reign, and reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem, and did not that which was right in the sight of the Lord his God, like David his father.
text.NABRE: Ahaz was twenty years old when he became king, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem. He did not do what was right in the sight of the Lord his God, as David his father had done.
text.DRB: Twenty years old was Achaz when he began to reign, and he reigned sixteen years in Jerusalem: he did not that which was pleasing in the sight of our Lord his God, as David his father.

text.NRSV: but he walked in the way of the kings of Israel. He even made his son pass through fire, according to the abominable practices of the nations whom the Lord drove out before the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: but walked in the way of the kings of Israel,—moreover also, me made, his son, pass through the fire, according to the abominable practices of the nations, whom Yahweh dispossessed from before the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: But he walked in the way of the kings of Israel, yea, and made his son to pass through the fire, according to the abominations of the heathen, whom the Lord cast out from before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: He walked in the way of the kings of Israel; he even immolated his child by fire, in accordance with the abominable practices of the nations whom the Lord had dispossessed before the Israelites.
text.DRB: But he walked in the way of the kings of Israel: moreover also he consecrated his son, making him pass through fire according to the idols of the Gentiles: which our Lord destroyed before the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: He sacrificed and made offerings on the high places, on the hills, and under every green tree.
text.Rotherham: and he offered sacrifice and burned incense in the high places, and on the hills,—and under every green tree.
text.KJV: And he sacrificed and burnt incense in the high places, and on the hills, and under every green tree.
text.NABRE: Further, he sacrificed and burned incense on the high places, on hills, and under every green tree.
text.DRB: He immolated also victims, and burnt incense in the excelses, and on the hills, and under every tree full of green leaves.

text.NRSV: Then King Rezin of Aram and King Pekah son of Remaliah of Israel came up to wage war on Jerusalem; they besieged Ahaz but could not conquer him.
text.Rotherham: then, came up Rezin king of Syria, and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel, unto Jerusalem, to make war,—and they laid siege against Ahaz, but could not overcome [him].
text.KJV: Then Rezin king of Syria and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel came up to Jerusalem to war: and they besieged Ahaz, but could not overcome him.
text.NABRE: Then Rezin, king of Aram, and Pekah, son of Remaliah, king of Israel, came up to Jerusalem to attack it. Although they besieged Ahaz, they were unable to do battle.
text.DRB: Then went up Rasin the king of Syria, & Phacee the son of Romelia the king of Israel into Jerusalem to fight: and when they besieged Achaz, they were not able to overcome him.

text.NRSV: At that time the king of Edom recovered Elath for Edom, and drove the Judeans from Elath; and the Edomites came to Elath, where they live to this day.
text.Rotherham: At that time, Rezin king of Syria recovered Elath, to Syria, and wholly cleared out the Jews from Eloth,—and, the Syrians, entered Elath, and have dwelt there, unto this day.
text.KJV: At that time Rezin king of Syria recovered Elath to Syria, and drave the Jews from Elath: and the Syrians came to Elath, and dwelt there unto this day.
text.NABRE: (In those days Rezin, king of Aram, recovered Elath for Aram, and drove the Judahites out of it. The Edomites then entered Elath, which they have occupied until the present.)
text.DRB: At that time Rasin the king of Syria restored Aila to Syria, and threw out the Jews out of Aila: and the Idumeians came into Aila, and dwelt there unto this day.

text.NRSV: Ahaz sent messengers to King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria, saying, “I am your servant and your son. Come up, and rescue me from the hand of the king of Aram and from the hand of the king of Israel, who are attacking me.”
text.Rotherham: So Ahaz sent messengers unto Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria, saying, Thy servant and thy son, I am,—Come up and save me, out of the hand of the king of Syria, and out of the hand of the king of Israel, who are rising up against me.
text.KJV: So Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath–pileser king of Assyria, saying, I am thy servant and thy son: come up, and save me out of the hand of the king of Syria, and out of the hand of the king of Israel, which rise up against me.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria, with the plea: “I am your servant and your son. Come up and rescue me from the power of the king of Aram and the king of Israel, who are attacking me.”
text.DRB: And Achaz sent messengers to Theglathphalasar the king of the Assyrians, saying: I am thy servant, and thy son: come up, and save me out of the hand of the king of Syria, and from the hand of the king of Israel, which are risen together against me.

text.NRSV: Ahaz also took the silver and gold found in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king’s house, and sent a present to the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: And Ahaz took the silver and the gold that was found in the house of Yahweh, and in the treasuries of the house of the king,—and sent them to the king of Assyria, as a bribe.
text.KJV: And Ahaz took the silver and gold that was found in the house of the Lord, and in the treasures of the king’s house, and sent it for a present to the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: Ahaz took the silver and gold that were in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king’s house and sent them as a present to the king of Assyria.
text.DRB: And when he had gathered together the silver and gold, that could be found in the house of our Lord, & in the king's treasures, he sent gifts to the king of the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: The king of Assyria listened to him; the king of Assyria went up against Damascus, and took it, carrying its people captive to Kir; then he killed Rezin.
text.Rotherham: So the king of Assyria hearkened unto him, and the king of Assyria came up unto Damascus, and seized it, and carried away the people thereof captive to Kir,—and put Rezin, to death.
text.KJV: And the king of Assyria hearkened unto him: for the king of Assyria went up against Damascus, and took it, and carried the people of it captive to Kir, and slew Rezin.
text.NABRE: The king of Assyria listened to him and moved against Damascus, captured it, deported its inhabitants to Kir, and put Rezin to death.
text.DRB: Who also agreed to his will: for the king of the Assyrians went up into Damascus, and wasted it: and he transferred the inhabitants thereof to Cyrene, but Rasin he slew.

text.NRSV: When King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet King Tiglath-pileser of Assyria, he saw the altar that was at Damascus. King Ahaz sent to the priest Urijah a model of the altar, and its pattern, exact in all its details.
text.Rotherham: So then King Ahaz went to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria, in Damascus, and saw the altar which was in Damascus,—and King Ahaz, sent unto Urijah the priest, a likeness of the altar, and a model thereof, according to all the workmanship thereof;
text.KJV: And king Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath–pileser king of Assyria, and saw an altar that was at Damascus: and king Ahaz sent to Urijah the priest the fashion of the altar, and the pattern of it, according to all the workmanship thereof.
text.NABRE: King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser, king of Assyria. When he saw the altar in Damascus, King Ahaz sent to Uriah the priest a model of the altar and a detailed design of its construction.
text.DRB: And king Achaz went forth to meet Theglathphalasar the king of the Assyrians into Damascus, and when he had seen the altar of Damascus, king Achaz sent to Urias the Priest a pattern of it, and a similitude according to all the work thereof,

text.NRSV: The priest Urijah built the altar; in accordance with all that King Ahaz had sent from Damascus, just so did the priest Urijah build it, before King Ahaz arrived from Damascus.
text.Rotherham: and Urijah the priest built an altar,—according to all that King Ahaz sent from Damascus, so, did Urijah the priest make it, by the time King Ahaz came from Damascus.
text.KJV: And Urijah the priest built an altar according to all that king Ahaz had sent from Damascus: so Urijah the priest made it against king Ahaz came from Damascus.
text.NABRE: Uriah the priest built an altar according to the plans which King Ahaz sent him from Damascus, and had it completed by the time King Ahaz returned from Damascus.
text.DRB: And Urias the Priest built an altar, according to all things, which king Achaz had commanded, from Damascus, so did the Priest Urias, whilst king Achaz came from Damascus.

text.NRSV: When the king came from Damascus, the king viewed the altar. Then the king drew near to the altar, offered on it his burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: And, when the king came from Damascus, then the king saw the altar,—so the king drew near unto the altar, and caused offerings to ascend thereupon.
text.KJV: And when the king was come from Damascus, the king saw the altar: and the king approached to the altar, and offered thereon.
text.NABRE: On his arrival from Damascus, the king inspected the altar; the king approached the altar, went up
text.DRB: And when the king was come from Damascus, he saw the altar and worshipped it: and went up and immolated holocausts, and his sacrifice,

text.NRSV: and his grain offering, poured his drink offering, and dashed the blood of his offerings of well-being against the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he made perfume with his ascending-sacrifice, and with his meal-offering, and poured out his drink-offering,—and dashed the blood of the peace-offerings which he had, upon the altar.
text.KJV: And he burnt his burnt offering and his meat offering, and poured his drink offering, and sprinkled the blood of his peace offerings, upon the altar.
text.NABRE: and sacrificed his burnt offering and grain offering, pouring out his libation, and sprinkling the blood of his communion offerings on the altar.
text.DRB: & offered libaments, & poured the blood of the pacifics, which he had offered upon the altar.

text.NRSV: The bronze altar that was before the Lord he removed from the front of the house, from the place between his altar and the house of the Lord, and put it on the north side of his altar.
text.Rotherham: And, the altar of bronze which was before Yahweh, he brought away from the forefront of the house, from between the altar, and the house of Yahweh,—and put it at the side of the altar northward.
text.KJV: And he brought also the brasen altar, which was before the Lord, from the forefront of the house, from between the altar and the house of the Lord, and put it on the north side of the altar.
text.NABRE: The bronze altar that stood before the Lord he brought from the front of the temple—that is, from the space between the new altar and the house of the Lord —and set it on the north side of his altar.
text.DRB: Moreover the altar of brass that was before our Lord, he removed from the face of the temple, and from the place of the altar, and from the place of the temple of our Lord: and he set it at the side of the altar toward the North.

text.NRSV: King Ahaz commanded the priest Urijah, saying, “Upon the great altar offer the morning burnt offering, and the evening grain offering, and the king’s burnt offering, and his grain offering, with the burnt offering of all the people of the land, their grain offering and their drink offering; then dash against it all the blood of the burnt offering, and all the blood of the sacrifice; but the bronze altar shall be for me to inquire by.”
text.Rotherham: And King Ahaz commanded Urijah the priest, saying—Upon the great altar, make thou perfume with the ascending-sacrifice of the morning, and with the meal-offering of the evening, and with the ascending-sacrifice of the king and with his meal-offering, and with the ascending-sacrifice of all the people of the land, and their meal-offering and their drink-offering, and, all the blood of the ascending-offering, and all the blood of the [peace] offering, thereupon, shalt thou dash,—but, the altar of bronze, shall be for me to inquire [into].
text.KJV: And king Ahaz commanded Urijah the priest, saying, Upon the great altar burn the morning burnt offering, and the evening meat offering, and the king’s burnt sacrifice, and his meat offering, with the burnt offering of all the people of the land, and their meat offering, and their drink offerings; and sprinkle upon it all the blood of the burnt offering, and all the blood of the sacrifice: and the brasen altar shall be for me to enquire by.
text.NABRE: King Ahaz commanded Uriah the priest, “Upon the large altar sacrifice the morning burnt offering and the evening grain offering, the king’s burnt offering and grain offering, and the burnt offering and grain offering of the people of the land. Their libations you must sprinkle on it along with all the blood of burnt offerings and sacrifices. But the old bronze altar shall be mine for consultation.”
text.DRB: King Achaz also commanded Urias the Priest, saying: Upon the greater altar offer the morning holocaust, and the evening sacrifice, and the king's holocaust, and his sacrifice, and the holocaust of the whole people of the land, and their sacrifices, and their libaments: and all the blood of the holocaust, and all the blood of the victim thou shalt pour out upon it: but the altar of brass shall be prepared ready at my pleasure.

text.NRSV: The priest Urijah did everything just as King Ahaz commanded.
text.Rotherham: So Urijah the priest did according to all that King Ahaz commanded.
text.KJV: Thus did Urijah the priest, according to all that king Ahaz commanded.
text.NABRE: Uriah the priest did just as King Ahaz had commanded.
text.DRB: Urias therefore the Priest did according to all things, which king Achaz had commanded him.

text.NRSV: Then King Ahaz cut off the frames of the stands, and removed the laver from them; he removed the sea from the bronze oxen that were under it, and put it on a pediment of stone.
text.Rotherham: And King Ahaz cut off the side walls of the stands, and took away from off them the laver, and, the sea, took he down from off the oxen of bronze, which were under it,—and set it on a pavement of stones.
text.KJV: And king Ahaz cut off the borders of the bases, and removed the laver from off them; and took down the sea from off the brasen oxen that were under it, and put it upon a pavement of stones.
text.NABRE: King Ahaz detached the panels from the stands and removed the basins from them; he also took down the bronze sea from the bronze oxen that supported it, and set it on a stone pavement.
text.DRB: And king Achaz took the engraven feet, and the lavatory that was upon them: and he took down the sea from the oxen of brass, that held it up, and put it upon the pavement paved with stone.

text.NRSV: The covered portal for use on the sabbath that had been built inside the palace, and the outer entrance for the king he removed from the house of the Lord. He did this because of the king of Assyria.
text.Rotherham: Also, the covered walk for the Sabbath, which they had built in the house, and the outer entrance for the king, he changed in the house of Yahweh,—because of the king of Assyria.
text.KJV: And the covert for the sabbath that they had built in the house, and the king’s entry without, turned he from the house of the Lord for the king of Assyria.
text.NABRE: In deference to the king of Assyria he removed the sabbath canopy that had been set up in the house of the Lord and the king’s outside entrance to the temple.
text.DRB: The Musach also of the Sabbath, which he had built in the temple: and the entry of the king outward he turned into the temple of our Lord, because of the king of the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Ahaz that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Ahaz, what he did, is, it, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Ahaz which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Ahaz, with what he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Achaz, which he did, are not these written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: Ahaz slept with his ancestors, and was buried with his ancestors in the city of David; then his son Hezekiah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Ahaz slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers, in the city of David,—and, Hezekiah his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Ahaz slept with his fathers, and was buried with his fathers in the city of David: and Hezekiah his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Ahaz rested with his ancestors; he was buried with his ancestors in the City of David, and his son Hezekiah succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Achaz slept with his fathers, and was buried with them in the city of David, and Ezechias his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In the twelfth year of King Ahaz of Judah, Hoshea son of Elah began to reign in Samaria over Israel; he reigned nine years.
text.Rotherham: In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah, began Hosea son of Elah to reign in Samaria, over Israel, [and he reigned] nine years.
text.KJV: In the twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah began Hoshea the son of Elah to reign in Samaria over Israel nine years.
text.NABRE: In the twelfth year of Ahaz, king of Judah, Hoshea, son of Elah, became king in Samaria over Israel for nine years.
text.DRB: In the twelfth year of Achaz king of Juda, reigned Osee the son of Ela in Samaria over Israel nine years.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, yet not like the kings of Israel who were before him.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—only, not like the kings of Israel who were before him.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord, but not as the kings of Israel that were before him.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, yet not to the extent of the kings of Israel before him.
text.DRB: And he did evil before our Lord: but not as the kings of Israel, that had been before him.

text.NRSV: King Shalmaneser of Assyria came up against him; Hoshea became his vassal, and paid him tribute.
text.Rotherham: Against him, came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria,—and Hoshea became his servant, and rendered him a present.
text.KJV: Against him came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria; and Hoshea became his servant, and gave him presents.
text.NABRE: Shalmaneser, king of Assyria, advanced against him, and Hoshea became his vassal and paid him tribute.
text.DRB: Against him came up Salmanasar king of the Assyrians, and Osee was made servant to him, and payed him tributes.

text.NRSV: But the king of Assyria found treachery in Hoshea; for he had sent messengers to King So of Egypt, and offered no tribute to the king of Assyria, as he had done year by year; therefore the king of Assyria confined him and imprisoned him.
text.Rotherham: Then found the king of Assyria, in Hosea, a conspiracy, in that he had sent messengers unto So king of Egypt, and had not brought up a present to the king of Assyria, as [he had done] year by year,—therefore the king of Assyria shut him up, and bound him in prison.
text.KJV: And the king of Assyria found conspiracy in Hoshea: for he had sent messengers to So king of Egypt, and brought no present to the king of Assyria, as he had done year by year: therefore the king of Assyria shut him up, and bound him in prison.
text.NABRE: But the king of Assyria found Hoshea guilty of conspiracy for sending messengers to the king of Egypt at Sais, and for failure to pay the annual tribute to the king of Assyria. So the king of Assyria arrested and imprisoned him.
text.DRB: And when the king of the Assyrians had found, that Osee endeavouring to rebel had sent messengers to Sua the king of Ægypt, that he might not pay tributes to the king of the Assyrians, as every year he was accustomed, he besieged him, and cast him bound into prison.

text.NRSV: Then the king of Assyria invaded all the land and came to Samaria; for three years he besieged it.
text.Rotherham: And the king of Assyria came up throughout all the land,—yea he came up to Samaria, and besieged it three years.
text.KJV: Then the king of Assyria came up throughout all the land, and went up to Samaria, and besieged it three years.
text.NABRE: Then the king of Assyria occupied the whole land and attacked Samaria, which he besieged for three years.
text.DRB: And he ranged through all the land: and going up to Samaria, he besieged it three years.

text.NRSV: In the ninth year of Hoshea the king of Assyria captured Samaria; he carried the Israelites away to Assyria. He placed them in Halah, on the Habor, the river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.
text.Rotherham: In the ninth year of Hoshea, did the king of Assyria capture Samaria, and carried Israel away captive to Assyria,—and settled them in Halah, and in Habor by the river of Gozan, and the mountains of Media.
text.KJV: In the ninth year of Hoshea the king of Assyria took Samaria, and carried Israel away into Assyria, and placed them in Halah and in Habor by the river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.
text.NABRE: X. The End of Israel Israelites Deported. In Hoshea’s ninth year, the king of Assyria took Samaria, deported the Israelites to Assyria, and settled them in Halah, and at the Habor, a river of Gozan, and in the cities of the Medes.
text.DRB: And in the ninth year of Osee, the king of Assyrians took Samaria, and transferred Israel unto the Assyrians: and he put them in Hala, & in Habor beside the river of Gozan, in the cities of the Medes.

text.NRSV: This occurred because the people of Israel had sinned against the Lord their God, who had brought them up out of the land of Egypt from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt. They had worshipped other gods
text.Rotherham: And thus it came to pass that Israel sinned against Yahweh their God, who brought them up out of the land of Egypt, from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt,—yea they did reverence to other gods;
text.KJV: For so it was, that the children of Israel had sinned against the Lord their God, which had brought them up out of the land of Egypt, from under the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt, and had feared other gods,
text.NABRE: This came about because the Israelites sinned against the Lord, their God, who had brought them up from the land of Egypt, from under the hand of Pharaoh, king of Egypt. They venerated other gods,
text.DRB: For it came to pass, when the children of Israel had sinned to our Lord their God, which brought them out of the land of Ægypt, & out of the hand of Pharao the king of Ægypt, they worshipped strange gods.

text.NRSV: and walked in the customs of the nations whom the Lord drove out before the people of Israel, and in the customs that the kings of Israel had introduced.
text.Rotherham: and walked in the statutes of the nations, whom Yahweh had dispossessed from before the sons of Israel,—and [in the statutes] of the kings of Israel, which they had made.
text.KJV: And walked in the statutes of the heathen, whom the Lord cast out from before the children of Israel, and of the kings of Israel, which they had made.
text.NABRE: they followed the rites of the nations whom the Lord had dispossessed before the Israelites and those that the kings of Israel had practiced.
