text.NRSV: Whom have you mocked and reviled? Against whom have you raised your voice and haughtily lifted your eyes? Against the Holy One of Israel!
text.Rotherham: Whom, hast thou reproached, and insulted? and, against whom, hast thou lifted high thy voice? yea thou hast proudly raised thine eyes, against the Holy One of Israel.
text.KJV: Whom hast thou reproached and blasphemed? and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thine eyes on high? even against the Holy One of Israel.
text.NABRE: Whom have you insulted and blasphemed, at whom have you raised your voice And lifted up your eyes on high? At the Holy One of Israel!
text.DRB: Whom hast thou upbraided, & whom hast thou blasphemed? Against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thine eyes in high? Against the holy one of Israel.

text.NRSV: By your messengers you have mocked the Lord, and you have said, ‘With my many chariots I have gone up the heights of the mountains, to the far recesses of Lebanon; I felled its tallest cedars, its choicest cypresses; I entered its farthest retreat, its densest forest.
text.Rotherham: Through thy messengers, thou hast reproached My Lord, and hast said—With my multitude of chariots, have I ascended the height of the mountains, the recesses of Lebanon,—and have cut down its tallest cedars, its choicest firs, and have entered the shelter of its summit, its thick garden forests.
text.KJV: By thy messengers thou hast reproached the Lord, and hast said, With the multitude of my chariots I am come up to the height of the mountains, to the sides of Lebanon, and will cut down the tall cedar trees thereof, and the choice fir trees thereof: and I will enter into the lodgings of his borders, and into the forest of his Carmel.
text.NABRE: Through the mouths of your messengers you insulted the Lord when you said, ‘With my many chariots I went up to the tops of the peaks, to the recesses of Lebanon, To cut down its lofty cedars, its choice cypresses; I reached to the farthest shelter, the forest ranges.
text.DRB: By the hand of thy servants thou hast upbraided our Lord, and hast said: In the multitude of my chariots have I climbed the height of mountains, in the top of Libanus, and have cut down high cedars thereof, and the chosen fir-trees of it. And I have entered into the borders thereof, & the forest of the carmel thereof

text.NRSV: I dug wells and drank foreign waters, I dried up with the sole of my foot all the streams of Egypt.’
text.Rotherham: I, have digged, and have drunk foreign waters,—and have dried up, with the soles of my feet, all the streams of besieged places.
text.KJV: I have digged and drunk strange waters, and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of besieged places.
text.NABRE: I myself dug wells and drank foreign waters, Drying up all the rivers of Egypt beneath the soles of my feet.’
text.DRB: have I cut down. And I have drunk strange waters, and have dried up with the steps of my feet all the waters inclosed.

text.NRSV: Have you not heard that I determined it long ago? I planned from days of old what now I bring to pass, that you should make fortified cities crash into heaps of ruins,
text.Rotherham: Hast thou not heard—that, long ago, that, is what I appointed, and, from days of old, devised it? Now, have I brought it to pass, that thou mightest serve to lay waste, in desolate ruins, fortified cities;
text.KJV: Hast thou not heard long ago how I have done it, and of ancient times that I have formed it? now have I brought it to pass, that thou shouldest be to lay waste fenced cities into ruinous heaps.
text.NABRE: “Have you not heard? A long time ago I prepared it, from days of old I planned it. Now I have brought it about: You are here to reduce fortified cities to heaps of ruins,
text.DRB: Why, hast thou not heard what I have done from the beginning? From ancient days I have made that thing, and now I have brought it to effect: and fenced cities shall be into ruin of little hills bickering together.

text.NRSV: while their inhabitants, shorn of strength, are dismayed and confounded; they have become like plants of the field and like tender grass, like grass on the housetops, blighted before it is grown.
text.Rotherham: And, their inhabitants, being powerless, were overthrown and put to shame,—they became grass of the field, and young herbage, grass on housetops, and seed withered before it came up.
text.KJV: Therefore their inhabitants were of small power, they were dismayed and confounded; they were as the grass of the field, and as the green herb, as the grass on the housetops, and as corn blasted before it be grown up.
text.NABRE: Their people powerless, dismayed and distraught. They are plants of the field, green growth, thatch on the rooftops, Grain scorched by the east wind.
text.DRB: And they that sit in them, weak of hand, they have trembled & are confounded, they became as the grass of the field, and the green herb of the roofs of houses, which withered before it came to ripeness.

text.NRSV: But I know your rising and your sitting, your going out and your coming in, and your raging against me.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, thine abode, and thy coming out and thy going in, I know,—and thy raging against me.
text.KJV: But I know thy abode, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy rage against me.
text.NABRE: I know when you stand or sit, when you come or go and how you rage against me.
text.DRB: Thy habitation, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy way I have known before, and thy fury against me.

text.NRSV: Because you have raged against me and your arrogance has come to my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth; I will turn you back on the way by which you came.
text.Rotherham: Because, thy raging against me, and thy contempt, have come up into mine ears, therefore will I put my ring in thy nose, and my bit in thy lips, and will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.
text.KJV: Because thy rage against me and thy tumult is come up into mine ears, therefore I will put my hook in thy nose, and my bridle in thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.
text.NABRE: Because you rage against me, and your smugness has reached my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth, And make you leave by the way you came.
text.DRB: Thou hast been mad against me, and thy pride hath ascended into mine ears: I therefore will put a ring in thy nostrils, and a bit in thy lips, and will bring thee back into the way, by the which thou camest.

text.NRSV: And this shall be the sign for you: This year you shall eat what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs from that; then in the third year sow, reap, plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.
text.Rotherham: And, this unto thee, is the sign—Eating, this year, the growth of scattered seeds, and, in the second year, that which groweth after,—then, in the third year, sow ye and reap, plant ye vineyards, and eat the fruit thereof;
text.KJV: And this shall be a sign unto thee, Ye shall eat this year such things as grow of themselves, and in the second year that which springeth of the same; and in the third year sow ye, and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat the fruits thereof.
text.NABRE: “This shall be a sign for you: This year you shall eat the aftergrowth, next year, what grows of itself; But in the third year, sow and reap, plant vineyards and eat their fruit!
text.DRB: And to thee Ezechias this shall be a sign: Eat this year what thou shalt find: and in the second year, the things that grow of themselves: moreover in the third year sow ye and reap: plant ye vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.

text.NRSV: The surviving remnant of the house of Judah shall again take root downward, and bear fruit upward;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the escaped of the house of Judah that remain, again—take root downward, and bear fruit upward;
text.KJV: And the remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah shall yet again take root downward, and bear fruit upward.
text.NABRE: The remaining survivors of the house of Judah shall again strike root below and bear fruit above.
text.DRB: And whatsoever shall be left of the house of Juda, shall take root downward, and bear fruit upward.

text.NRSV: for from Jerusalem a remnant shall go out, and from Mount Zion a band of survivors. The zeal of the Lord of hosts will do this.
text.Rotherham: For, out of Jerusalem, shall go forth a remnant, and that which hath escaped—out of Mount Zion,—the jealousy of Yahweh of hosts, will perform this.
text.KJV: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and they that escape out of mount Zion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.
text.NABRE: For out of Jerusalem shall come a remnant, and from Mount Zion, survivors. The zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.
text.DRB: For out of Jerusalem there shall remnants go forth, and that which is to be saved from the mount of Sion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.

text.NRSV: Therefore thus says the Lord concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city, shoot an arrow there, come before it with a shield, or cast up a siege mound against it.
text.Rotherham: Therefore—Thus, saith Yahweh, concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not enter this city, nor shoot there, an arrow,—nor attack it with shield, nor cast up against it, a mound;
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come before it with shield, nor cast a bank against it.
text.NABRE: “Therefore, thus says the Lord about the king: He shall not come as far as this city, nor shoot there an arrow, nor confront it with a shield, Nor cast up a siege-work against it.
text.DRB: Wherefore thus saith our Lord of the king of the Assyrians: He shall not enter into this city, nor shoot arrow into it, neither shall shield occupy it, nor munition compass it.

text.NRSV: By the way that he came, by the same he shall return; he shall not come into this city, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: By the way that he cometh in, by the same, shall he return,—and, into this city, shall he not enter, declareth Yahweh!
text.KJV: By the way that he came, by the same shall he return, and shall not come into this city, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: By the way he came he shall leave, never coming as far as this city, oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: By the way, that he came, he shall return: and into this city he shall not enter, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: For I will defend this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of my servant David.”
text.Rotherham: Thus will I throw a covering over this city, to save it,—for mine own sake, and for the sake of David my servant.
text.KJV: For I will defend this city, to save it, for mine own sake, and for my servant David’s sake.
text.NABRE: I will shield and save this city for my own sake and the sake of David my servant.”
text.DRB: And I will protect this city, and will save it for myself, and for David my servant.

text.NRSV: That very night the angel of the Lord set out and struck down one hundred eighty-five thousand in the camp of the Assyrians; when morning dawned, they were all dead bodies.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, during that night, that the messenger of Yahweh went forth, and smote, in the camp of the Assyrians, a hundred and eighty-five thousand. And, when men arose early in the morning, lo! they were all, dead bodies!
text.KJV: And it came to pass that night, that the angel of the Lord went out, and smote in the camp of the Assyrians an hundred fourscore and five thousand: and when they arose early in the morning, behold, they were all dead corpses.
text.NABRE: That night the angel of the Lord went forth and struck down one hundred and eighty-five thousand men in the Assyrian camp. Early the next morning, there they were, dead, all those corpses!
text.DRB: It came to pass therefore in that night, an Angel of our Lord came, and struck in the camp of the Assyrians an hundred eighty five thousand. And when he was risen early, he saw all the bodies of the dead,

text.NRSV: Then King Sennacherib of Assyria left, went home, and lived at Nineveh.
text.Rotherham: So Sennacherib king of Assyria brake up, and went his way, and returned,—and remained in Nineveh.
text.KJV: So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed, and went and returned, and dwelt at Nineveh.
text.NABRE: So Sennacherib, the king of Assyria, broke camp, departed, returned home, and stayed in Nineveh.
text.DRB: and Sennacherib the king of the Assyrians departing went away, and tarried in Ninive.

text.NRSV: As he was worshipping in the house of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer killed him with the sword, and they escaped into the land of Ararat. His son Esar-haddon succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he was bowing down in the house of Nisroch his god, that, Adrammelech and Sharezer [his sons], smote him with the sword, howbeit, they, escaped into the land of Ararat,—and Esarhaddon his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as he was worshipping in the house of Nisroch his god, that Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons smote him with the sword: and they escaped into the land of Armenia. And Esarhaddon his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: When he was worshiping in the temple of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer struck him down with the sword and fled into the land of Ararat. His son Esarhaddon reigned in his place.
text.DRB: And when he adored in the temple of Nesroch his god, Adramelech and Sarasar his sons struck him with the sword, and they fled into the land of the Armenians, and Asarhaddon his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz came to him, and said to him, “Thus says the Lord: Set your house in order, for you shall die; you shall not recover.”
text.Rotherham: In those days, was Hezekiah sick, unto death,—and Isaiah son of Amoz, the prophet, came unto him, and said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh Set in order thy house, for, about to die, thou, art, and shalt not recover.
text.KJV: In those days was Hezekiah sick unto death. And the prophet Isaiah the son of Amoz came to him, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Set thine house in order; for thou shalt die, and not live.
text.NABRE: In those days, when Hezekiah was mortally ill, the prophet Isaiah, son of Amoz, came and said to him: “Thus says the Lord: Put your house in order, for you are about to die; you shall not recover.”
text.DRB: In those days Ezechias was sick even to death: & Isaie the son of Amos the prophet came and said to him: Thus saith our Lord God: Take order with thy house, for thou shalt die, and shalt not live.

text.NRSV: Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord:
text.Rotherham: Then he turned his face unto the wall,—and prayed unto Yahweh, saying:
text.KJV: Then he turned his face to the wall, and prayed unto the Lord, saying,
text.NABRE: He turned his face to the wall and prayed to the Lord:
text.DRB: Who turned his face to the wall, and prayed our Lord, saying:

text.NRSV: “Remember now, O Lord, I implore you, how I have walked before you in faithfulness with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your sight.” Hezekiah wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: I beseech thee, O Yahweh, remember, I pray thee, how I have walked before thee, in truth, and with a whole heart, and, that which is good in thine eyes, have I done! And Hezekiah wept aloud.
text.KJV: I beseech thee, O Lord, remember now how I have walked before thee in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Hezekiah wept sore.
text.NABRE: “Ah, Lord, remember how faithfully and wholeheartedly I conducted myself in your presence, doing what was good in your sight!” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
text.DRB: I beseech thee Lord, remember I pray thee how I have walked before thee in truth, and in a perfect heart, and have done that which is liked before thee. Ezechias therefore wept with great weeping.

text.NRSV: Before Isaiah had gone out of the middle court, the word of the Lord came to him:
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass that Isaiah, had not gone out into the middle court, when the word of Yahweh came unto him, saying:
text.KJV: And it came to pass, afore Isaiah was gone out into the middle court, that the word of the Lord came to him, saying,
text.NABRE: Before Isaiah had left the central courtyard, the word of the Lord came to him:
text.DRB: And before Isaie was gone out of the midst of the court, the word of our Lord came to him, saying:

text.NRSV: “Turn back, and say to Hezekiah prince of my people, Thus says the Lord, the God of your ancestor David: I have heard your prayer, I have seen your tears; indeed, I will heal you; on the third day you shall go up to the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Return, and thou shalt say unto Hezekiah the leader of my people—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of David thy father, I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears,—Behold me! about to heal thee, On the third day, shalt thou go up unto the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: Turn again, and tell Hezekiah the captain of my people, Thus saith the Lord, the God of David thy father, I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears: behold, I will heal thee: on the third day thou shalt go up unto the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Go back and tell Hezekiah, the leader of my people: “Thus says the Lord, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Now I am healing you. On the third day you shall go up to the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: Return, and tell Ezechias the Prince of my people: Thus saith our Lord the God of David thy father: I have heard thy prayer, and seen thy tears: and behold I have healed thee, the third day thou shalt go up to the temple of the Lord.

text.NRSV: I will add fifteen years to your life. I will deliver you and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria; I will defend this city for my own sake and for my servant David’s sake.”
text.Rotherham: And I will add unto thy days, fifteen years, and, out of the hand of the king of Assyria, will I deliver thee, and this city,—and will throw a covering over this city, for my own sake, and for the sake of David my servant.
text.KJV: And I will add unto thy days fifteen years; and I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria; and I will defend this city for mine own sake, and for my servant David’s sake.
text.NABRE: I will add to your life fifteen years. I will rescue you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria; I will be a shield to this city for my own sake and the sake of David my servant.”
text.DRB: And I will add to thy days fifteen years: yea & out of the hand of the king of Assyrians I will deliver thee, and this city, and I will protect this city for my sake, and for David my servant.

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah said, “Bring a lump of figs.” They brought it, and applied it to the inflammation, and he recovered.
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah said—Take ye a cake of figs. So they took and laid it upon the boil, and he recovered,
text.KJV: And Isaiah said, Take a lump of figs. And they took and laid it on the boil, and he recovered.
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah said, “Bring a poultice of figs and apply it to the boil for his recovery.”
text.DRB: And Isaie said: Fetch me a bunch of figs. Which when they had brought, and had laid it upon his sore, he was cured.

text.NRSV: Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “What shall be the sign that the Lord will heal me, and that I shall go up to the house of the Lord on the third day?”
text.Rotherham: Now Hezekiah had said unto Isaiah, What sign is there, that Yahweh will heal me,—and that I shall go up on the third day, to the house of Yahweh?
text.KJV: And Hezekiah said unto Isaiah, What shall be the sign that the Lord will heal me, and that I shall go up into the house of the Lord the third day?
text.NABRE: Hezekiah asked Isaiah, “What is the sign that the Lord will heal me and that I shall go up to the house of the Lord on the third day?”
text.DRB: And Ezechias had said to Isaie: What sign shall there be, that our Lord will heal me, and that I shall go up the third day to the temple of our Lord?

text.NRSV: Isaiah said, “This is the sign to you from the Lord, that the Lord will do the thing that he has promised: shall the shadow go forward ten intervals, or shall it go back ten intervals?”
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah said,—This unto thee, is the sign from Yahweh, that Yahweh will do the thing which he hath spoken, The shadow hath gone forward ten steps, shall it return ten steps?
text.KJV: And Isaiah said, This sign shalt thou have of the Lord, that the Lord will do the thing that he hath spoken: shall the shadow go forward ten degrees, or go back ten degrees?
text.NABRE: Isaiah replied, “This will be the sign for you from the Lord that he will carry out the word he has spoken: Shall the shadow go forward or back ten steps?”
text.DRB: To whom Isaie said: This shall be the sign from our Lord, that our Lord will do the word, which he hath spoken: Wilt thou that the shadow go forward ten times, or that it go back so many degrees.

text.NRSV: Hezekiah answered, “It is a light thing for the shadow to lengthen ten intervals; rather let the shadow go back ten intervals.”
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah said—It is, a light thing, for the shadow, to decline ten steps,—Nay, but let the shadow go back ten steps.
text.KJV: And Hezekiah answered, It is a light thing for the shadow to go down ten degrees: nay, but let the shadow return backward ten degrees.
text.NABRE: “It is easy for the shadow to advance ten steps,” Hezekiah answered. “Rather, let it go back ten steps.”
text.DRB: And Ezechias said: It is an easy matter for the shadow to go forward ten times, neither will I that this be done, but that it return back ten degrees.

text.NRSV: The prophet Isaiah cried to the Lord; and he brought the shadow back the ten intervals, by which it had declined on the dial of Ahaz.
text.Rotherham: And Isaiah the prophet cried unto Yahweh, and he caused the shadow on the steps by which it had gone down on the steps of Ahaz to go back, ten steps.
text.KJV: And Isaiah the prophet cried unto the Lord: and he brought the shadow ten degrees backward, by which it had gone down in the dial of Ahaz.
text.NABRE: So Isaiah the prophet invoked the Lord. He made the shadow go back the ten steps it had descended on the staircase to the terrace of Ahaz.
text.DRB: Isaie therefore the Prophet invocated our Lord, & brought back the shadow by the lines, by the which it was now gone down in the dial of Achaz, backward ten degrees.

text.NRSV: At that time Merodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent envoys with letters and a present to Hezekiah, for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick.
text.Rotherham: At that time, Berodach-baladan son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present, unto Hezekiah,—for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick.
text.KJV: At that time Berodach–baladan, the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present unto Hezekiah: for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick.
text.NABRE: At that time, Berodach-baladan, son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and gifts to Hezekiah when he heard that he had been ill.
text.DRB: In that time Berodach Baladan, the son of Baladan, the king of the Babylonians sent letters & gifts to Ezechias: for he had heard that Ezechias had been sick.

text.NRSV: Hezekiah welcomed them; he showed them all his treasure house, the silver, the gold, the spices, the precious oil, his armory, all that was found in his storehouses; there was nothing in his house or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah hearkened unto them, and shewed them all his house of precious things—the silver, and the gold, and the spices, and the precious ointment, and his armoury, and all that was found among his treasures,—there was, nothing, that Hezekiah shewed them not, in his house or in all his dominion.
text.KJV: And Hezekiah hearkened unto them, and shewed them all the house of his precious things, the silver, and the gold, and the spices, and the precious ointment, and all the house of his armour, and all that was found in his treasures: there was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominion, that Hezekiah shewed them not.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah listened to the envoys and then showed off his whole treasury: his silver, gold, spices and perfumed oil, his armory, and everything in his storerooms; there was nothing in his house or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
text.DRB: And Ezechias rejoiced in their coming, and he shewed them the house of aromatical spices, and gold and silver, and diverse precious odours, ointments also, and the house of his vessels, and all that he had in his treasures. There was not any thing which Ezechias shewed them not in his house, and in all his power.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Isaiah came to King Hezekiah, and said to him, “What did these men say? From where did they come to you?” Hezekiah answered, “They have come from a far country, from Babylon.”
text.Rotherham: Then came Isaiah the prophet unto King Hezekiah,—and said unto him—What said these men, and from whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, From a land far off, came they—from Babylon.
text.KJV: Then came Isaiah the prophet unto king Hezekiah, and said unto him, What said these men? and from whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, They are come from a far country, even from Babylon.
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah and asked him: “What did these men say to you? Where did they come from?” Hezekiah replied, “They came from a distant land, from Babylon.”
text.DRB: And Isaie the Prophet came to king Ezechias, and said to him: What said these men? Or from whence came they to thee? To whom Ezechias said: From a far country they came to me out of Babylon.

text.NRSV: He said, “What have they seen in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house; there is nothing in my storehouses that I did not show them.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—What have they seen in thy house? Then said Hezekiah—All that is in my house, have they seen, there was, nothing, that I shewed them not, among my treasures.
text.KJV: And he said, What have they seen in thine house? And Hezekiah answered, All the things that are in mine house have they seen: there is nothing among my treasures that I have not shewed them.
text.NABRE: He asked, “What did they see in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They saw everything in my house. There is nothing in my storerooms that I did not show them.”
text.DRB: But he answered: What saw they in thy house? Ezechias said: They saw all things whatsoever are in my house: there is nothing that I have not shewed them in my treasures.

text.NRSV: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the Lord:
text.Rotherham: Then said Isaiah unto Hezekiah,—Hear thou the word of Yahweh:
text.KJV: And Isaiah said unto Hezekiah, Hear the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah: “Hear the word of the Lord:
text.DRB: Isaie therefore said to Ezechias, hear the word of our Lord:

text.NRSV: Days are coming when all that is in your house, and that which your ancestors have stored up until this day, shall be carried to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Lo! days are coming, when all that is in thine house, and that thy fathers have treasured up, unto this day, shall be carried into Babylon,—nothing shall be left, saith Yahweh;
text.KJV: Behold, the days come, that all that is in thine house, and that which thy fathers have laid up in store unto this day, shall be carried into Babylon: nothing shall be left, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: The time is coming when all that is in your house, everything that your ancestors have stored up until this day, shall be carried off to Babylon; nothing shall be left, says the Lord.
text.DRB: Behold the days shall come, and all things shall be taken away, that are in thy house, and that thy fathers have laid up until this day, into Babylon: there shall not any thing remain, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Some of your own sons who are born to you shall be taken away; they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.”
text.Rotherham: and, of thy sons who shall issue from thee, whom thou shalt beget, shall they take away,—and they shall become eunuchs, in the palace of the king of Babylon.
text.KJV: And of thy sons that shall issue from thee, which thou shalt beget, shall they take away; and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
text.NABRE: Some of your own descendants, your offspring, your progeny, shall be taken and made attendants in the palace of the king of Babylon.”
text.DRB: Yea of the children also that come forth of thee, whom thou shalt beget shall be taken away, and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

text.NRSV: Then Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the Lord that you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “Why not, if there will be peace and security in my days?”
text.Rotherham: And Hezekiah said unto Isaiah—Good, is the word of Yahweh, which thou hast spoken. And he said—Is it not, that, peace and stability, there shall be in my days?
text.KJV: Then said Hezekiah unto Isaiah, Good is the word of the Lord which thou hast spoken. And he said, Is it not good, if peace and truth be in my days?
text.NABRE: Hezekiah replied to Isaiah, “The word of the Lord which you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “There will be peace and stability in my lifetime.”
text.DRB: Ezechias said to Isaie: The word of our Lord which thou hast spoken is good: be there peace and truth in my days.

text.NRSV: The rest of the acts of Hezekiah, all his might, and how he made the pool and the conduit and brought water into the city, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Hezekiah, and all his might, and how he made a pool and an aqueduct, and brought water into the city, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: And the rest of the acts of Hezekiah, and all his might, and how he made a pool, and a conduit, and brought water into the city, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Hezekiah, with all his valor, and how he constructed the pool and conduit and brought water into the city, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Ezechias, and all his strength, and how he made a pool, & a conduit, & brought waters into the city, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: Hezekiah slept with his ancestors; and his son Manasseh succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: So Hezekiah slept with his fathers, and, Manasseh his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Hezekiah slept with his fathers: and Manasseh his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Hezekiah rested with his ancestors, and his son Manasseh succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Ezechias slept with his fathers, and Manasses his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign; he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hephzibah.
text.Rotherham: Twelve years old, was Manasseh when he began to reign, and, fifty-five years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and the name of his mother was Hephzibah.
text.KJV: Manasseh was twelve years old when he began to reign, and reigned fifty and five years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Hephzi–bah.
text.NABRE: Manasseh was twelve years old when he became king, and he reigned fifty-five years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Hephzibah.
text.DRB: Twelve years old was Manasses, when he began to reign, & he reigned five and fifty years in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Haphsiba.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, following the abominable practices of the nations whom the Lord drove out before the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—after the abominable practices of the nations, whom Yahweh had dispossessed from before the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord, after the abominations of the heathen, whom the Lord cast out before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, following the abominable practices of the nations whom the Lord had dispossessed before the Israelites.
text.DRB: And he did evil in the sight of our Lord, according to the idols of the Nations, which our Lord destroyed from the face of the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: For he rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had destroyed; he erected altars for Baal, made a sacred pole, as King Ahab of Israel had done, worshipped all the host of heaven, and served them.
text.Rotherham: He again built the high places, which Hezekiah his father had destroyed,—and reared altars to Baal, and made a Sacred Stem, as did Ahab, king of Israel, and bowed down to all the army of the heavens, and served them.
text.KJV: For he built up again the high places which Hezekiah his father had destroyed; and he reared up altars for Baal, and made a grove, as did Ahab king of Israel; and worshipped all the host of heaven, and served them.
text.NABRE: He rebuilt the high places which Hezekiah his father had destroyed. He set up altars to Baal and also made an asherah, as Ahab, king of Israel, had done. He bowed down to the whole host of heaven and served them.
text.DRB: And he was turned, and built the excelses, which Ezechias his father had destroyed: and he set up altars to Baal, and made groves, as Achab the king of Israel had done: and he adored all the host of heaven, and worshipped it.

text.NRSV: He built altars in the house of the Lord, of which the Lord had said, “In Jerusalem I will put my name.”
text.Rotherham: And he used to build altars in the house of Yahweh,—concerning which Yahweh had said,—In Jerusalem, will I put my Name.
text.KJV: And he built altars in the house of the Lord, of which the Lord said, In Jerusalem will I put my name.
text.NABRE: He built altars in the house of the Lord, of which the Lord had said: In Jerusalem I will set my name.
text.DRB: And he built altars in the house of our Lord, of the which our Lord said: In Jerusalem I will put my name.

text.NRSV: He built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he built altars to all the army of the heavens,—in the two courts of the house of Yahweh;
text.KJV: And he built altars for all the host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: And he built altars for the whole host of heaven in the two courts of the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: And he built altars to all the host of heaven in the two courts of the temple of our Lord.

text.NRSV: He made his son pass through fire; he practiced soothsaying and augury, and dealt with mediums and with wizards. He did much evil in the sight of the Lord, provoking him to anger.
text.Rotherham: and caused his son to pass through the fire, and practised hidden arts and used divination, and dealt with a familiar spirit and wizards,—he exceeded in doing the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh, provoking [him] to anger;
text.KJV: And he made his son pass through the fire, and observed times, and used enchantments, and dealt with familiar spirits and wizards: he wrought much wickedness in the sight of the Lord, to provoke him to anger.
text.NABRE: He immolated his child by fire. He practiced soothsaying and divination, and reintroduced the consulting of ghosts and spirits. He did much evil in the Lord ’s sight and provoked him to anger.
text.DRB: And he made his son pass through fire: and he used soothsaying, and observed divinations, and made pythons, and multiplied enchanters, to do evil before our Lord, & to provoke him.

text.NRSV: The carved image of Asherah that he had made he set in the house of which the Lord said to David and to his son Solomon, “In this house, and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will put my name forever;
text.Rotherham: and he set the image of the Sacred Stem which he had made,—in the house, of which Yahweh had said unto David, and unto Solomon his son, In this house, and in Jerusalem which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, will I put my Name, unto times age-abiding;
text.KJV: And he set a graven image of the grove that he had made in the house, of which the Lord said to David, and to Solomon his son, In this house, and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all tribes of Israel, will I put my name for ever:
text.NABRE: The Asherah idol he had made, he placed in the Lord ’s house, of which the Lord had said to David and to his son Solomon: In this house and in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I shall set my name forever.
text.DRB: He set also the idol of the grove, which he had made in the temple of our Lord: concerning the which our Lord spake to David, and to Salomon his son: In this temple, & in Jerusalem, which I have chosen out of all the tribes of Israel, I will put my name for ever.

text.NRSV: I will not cause the feet of Israel to wander any more out of the land that I gave to their ancestors, if only they will be careful to do according to all that I have commanded them, and according to all the law that my servant Moses commanded them.”
text.Rotherham: and I will not again let the foot of Israel wander away from the soil which I gave unto their fathers. Only if they take heed to do according to all that I have commanded them, even to the extent of all the law which, my servant Moses, commanded them.
text.KJV: Neither will I make the feet of Israel move any more out of the land which I gave their fathers; only if they will observe to do according to all that I have commanded them, and according to all the law that my servant Moses commanded them.
text.NABRE: I will no longer make Israel step out of the land I gave their ancestors, provided that they are careful to observe all I have commanded them and the entire law which Moses my servant enjoined upon them.
text.DRB: And I will no more make the foot of Israel to be moved out of the land, which I gave to their fathers: yet so if in work they shall keep all things that I have commanded them, & all the law which my servant Moyses commanded them.

text.NRSV: But they did not listen; Manasseh misled them to do more evil than the nations had done whom the Lord destroyed before the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: But they hearkened not,—and Manasseh led them astray to do the thing that was wicked, more than the nations which Yahweh destroyed from before the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: But they hearkened not: and Manasseh seduced them to do more evil than did the nations whom the Lord destroyed before the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: But they did not listen. Manasseh misled them into doing even greater evil than the nations the Lord had destroyed at the coming of the Israelites.
text.DRB: But they heard not: but were seduced by Manasses, to do evil above the Nations, which our Lord destroyed before the face of the children of Israel

text.NRSV: The Lord said by his servants the prophets,
text.Rotherham: Yahweh therefore spake through his servants the prophets saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake by his servants the prophets, saying,
text.NABRE: Then the Lord spoke through his servants the prophets:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake in the hand of his servants the Prophets, saying:

text.NRSV: “Because King Manasseh of Judah has committed these abominations, has done things more wicked than all that the Amorites did, who were before him, and has caused Judah also to sin with his idols;
text.Rotherham: Because Manasseh king of Judah hath made these abominations—hath done that which is wicked, more than all which the Amorites did who were before him, and hath caused, even Judah, to sin with his manufactured gods,
text.KJV: Because Manasseh king of Judah hath done these abominations, and hath done wickedly above all that the Amorites did, which were before him, and hath made Judah also to sin with his idols:
text.NABRE: “Because Manasseh, king of Judah, has practiced these abominations, and has done greater evil than all that was done by the Amorites before him, and has led Judah into sin by his idols,
text.DRB: Because Manasses the king of Juda hath done these most wicked abominations, passing all things that the Amorrheites did before him, and hath made Juda also to sin in his filths:

text.NRSV: therefore thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, I am bringing upon Jerusalem and Judah such evil that the ears of everyone who hears of it will tingle.
text.Rotherham: therefore—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, Behold me! bringing in calamity upon Jerusalem and Judah,—that, whosoever heareth thereof, both his ears, will tingle;
text.KJV: Therefore thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Behold, I am bringing such evil upon Jerusalem and Judah, that whosoever heareth of it, both his ears shall tingle.
text.NABRE: therefore, thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: I am about to bring such evil on Jerusalem and Judah that, when any hear of it, their ears shall ring:
text.DRB: therefore thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: Behold I will bring in evils upon Jerusalem and Juda: that whosoever shall hear it, both his ears shall tingle.

text.NRSV: I will stretch over Jerusalem the measuring line for Samaria, and the plummet for the house of Ahab; I will wipe Jerusalem as one wipes a dish, wiping it and turning it upside down.
text.Rotherham: And I will stretch over Jerusalem, the line of Samaria, and the plummet of the house of Ahab,—and will wipe out Jerusalem, as one wipeth out a dish, wiping it and turning it upside down;
text.KJV: And I will stretch over Jerusalem the line of Samaria, and the plummet of the house of Ahab: and I will wipe Jerusalem as a man wipeth a dish, wiping it, and turning it upside down.
text.NABRE: I will measure Jerusalem with the same cord as I did Samaria, and with the plummet I used for the house of Ahab. I will wipe Jerusalem clean as one wipes a dish, wiping it inside and out.
text.DRB: And I will stretch out upon Jerusalem the cord of Samaria, and the weight of the house of Achab: and I will wipe out Jerusalem, as tables are wont to be wiped out, and wiping out I will turn it, and draw often the pencil upon the face thereof.

text.NRSV: I will abandon the remnant of my inheritance, and give them into the hand of their enemies; they shall become a prey and a spoil to all their enemies,
text.Rotherham: And will abandon the remnant of mine inheritance, and deliver them into the hand of their enemies,—and they shall become a prey and a plunder, to all their enemies:
text.KJV: And I will forsake the remnant of mine inheritance, and deliver them into the hand of their enemies; and they shall become a prey and a spoil to all their enemies;
text.NABRE: I will cast off the survivors of my inheritance. I will deliver them into enemy hands, to become prey and booty for all their enemies,
text.DRB: But I will leave remnants of mine inheritance, and will deliver them into the hands of their enemies: and they shall be unto waste, and unto spoil to all their adversaries:

text.NRSV: because they have done what is evil in my sight and have provoked me to anger, since the day their ancestors came out of Egypt, even to this day.”
text.Rotherham: Because they have done the thing that is wicked in mine eyes, and have become such as to provoke me to anger,—from the day when their fathers came forth out of Egypt, even until this day.
text.KJV: Because they have done that which was evil in my sight, and have provoked me to anger, since the day their fathers came forth out of Egypt, even unto this day.
text.NABRE: because they have done what is evil in my sight and provoked me from the day their ancestors came forth from Egypt until this very day.”
text.DRB: because they have done evil before me, and have continued provoking me, from the day that their fathers came out of Ægypt, until this day.

text.NRSV: Moreover Manasseh shed very much innocent blood, until he had filled Jerusalem from one end to another, besides the sin that he caused Judah to commit, so that they did what was evil in the sight of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Moreover also, innocent blood, did Manasseh shed in great abundance, till he had filled Jerusalem, from one end to the other, besides his committing the sins which he caused, Judah, to commit, in doing the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Moreover Manasseh shed innocent blood very much, till he had filled Jerusalem from one end to another; beside his sin wherewith he made Judah to sin, in doing that which was evil in the sight of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Manasseh shed so much innocent blood that it filled the length and breadth of Jerusalem, in addition to the sin he caused Judah to commit by doing what was evil in the Lord ’s sight.
text.DRB: Moreover Manasses shed also innocent blood exceeding much, till he filled Jerusalem even to the mouth: beside his sins, wherein he made Juda to sin, to do evil before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Manasseh, all that he did, and the sin that he committed, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Manasseh, and all that he did, and his sin that he committed, are, they, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Manasseh, and all that he did, and his sin that he sinned, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Manasseh, with all that he did and the sin he committed, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Manasses, and all that he did, and his sin, which he sinned, are not these things written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: Manasseh slept with his ancestors, and was buried in the garden of his house, in the garden of Uzza. His son Amon succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And Manasseh slept with his fathers, and was buried in the garden of his own house, in the garden of Uzza,—and, Amon his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And Manasseh slept with his fathers, and was buried in the garden of his own house, in the garden of Uzza: and Amon his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: Manasseh rested with his ancestors; he was buried in his palace garden, the garden of Uzza, and his son Amon succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And Manasses slept with his fathers, and was buried in the garden of his house, in the garden of Oza: and Amon his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Amon was twenty-two years old when he began to reign; he reigned two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz of Jotbah.
text.Rotherham: Twenty-two years old, was Amon when he began to reign, and, two years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, his mother’s name, was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz, of Jotbah.
text.KJV: Amon was twenty and two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned two years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Meshullemeth, the daughter of Haruz of Jotbah.
text.NABRE: Reign of Amon. Amon was twenty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned two years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Meshullemeth, daughter of Haruz, from Jotbah.
text.DRB: Two and twenty years old was Amon when he began to reign: two years also he reigned in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Messalemeth the daughter of Jeteba.

text.NRSV: He did what was evil in the sight of the Lord, as his father Manasseh had done.
text.Rotherham: And he did the thing that was wicked in the eyes of Yahweh,—as did Manasseh his father;
text.KJV: And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord, as his father Manasseh did.
text.NABRE: He did what was evil in the Lord ’s sight, as his father Manasseh had done.
text.DRB: And he did evil in the sight of our Lord, as Manasses his father had done.

text.NRSV: He walked in all the way in which his father walked, served the idols that his father served, and worshipped them;
text.Rotherham: yea he walked in all the way wherein, his father, had walked,—and served the manufactured gods that, his father, had served, and bowed down to them;
text.KJV: And he walked in all the way that his father walked in, and served the idols that his father served, and worshipped them:
text.NABRE: He walked in all the ways of his father; he served the idols his father had served, and bowed down to them.
text.DRB: And he walked in all the way, by the which his father had walked: and he served the filths, which his father had served, and he adored them,

text.NRSV: he abandoned the Lord, the God of his ancestors, and did not walk in the way of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and forsook Yahweh the God of his fathers,—and walked not in the way of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he forsook the Lord God of his fathers, and walked not in the way of the Lord.
text.NABRE: He abandoned the Lord, the God of his ancestors, and did not walk in the way of the Lord.
text.DRB: and forsook our Lord the God of his fathers, and walked not in the way of our Lord.

text.NRSV: The servants of Amon conspired against him and killed the king in his house.
text.Rotherham: And the servants of Amon, conspired against him,—and slew the king in his own house.
text.KJV: And the servants of Amon conspired against him, and slew the king in his own house.
text.NABRE: Officials of Amon plotted against him and killed the king in his palace,
text.DRB: And his servants lay in wait against him, and slew the king in his house.

text.NRSV: But the people of the land killed all those who had conspired against King Amon, and the people of the land made his son Josiah king to succeed him.
text.Rotherham: Then the people of the land smote all who had conspired against King Amon,—and the people of the land made, Josiah his son, king, in his stead.
text.KJV: And the people of the land slew all them that had conspired against king Amon; and the people of the land made Josiah his son king in his stead.
text.NABRE: but the people of the land then slew all who had plotted against King Amon, and the people of the land made his son Josiah king in his stead.
text.DRB: But the people of the land struck all them, that had conspired against king Amon: and made Josias his son their king for him.

text.NRSV: Now the rest of the acts of Amon that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Annals of the Kings of Judah?
text.Rotherham: Now, the rest of the story of Amon, what he did, is, it, not written in the book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Judah?
text.KJV: Now the rest of the acts of Amon which he did, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?
text.NABRE: The rest of the acts of Amon, which he did, are recorded in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah.
text.DRB: But the rest of the words of Amon which he did, are not these written in the Book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?

text.NRSV: He was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzza; then his son Josiah succeeded him.
text.Rotherham: And he was buried in his grave, in the garden of Uzza,—and, Josiah his son, reigned, in his stead.
text.KJV: And he was buried in his sepulchre in the garden of Uzza: and Josiah his son reigned in his stead.
text.NABRE: He was buried in his own grave in the garden of Uzza, and his son Josiah succeeded him as king.
text.DRB: And they buried him in his sepulchre, in the garden of Oza: and Josias his son reigned for him.

text.NRSV: Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign; he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah of Bozkath.
text.Rotherham: Eight years old, was Josiah when he began to reign, and, thirty-one years, reigned he in Jerusalem,—and, this mother’s name, was Jedidah daughter of Adaiah, of Bozkath.
text.KJV: Josiah was eight years old when he began to reign, and he reigned thirty and one years in Jerusalem. And his mother’s name was Jedidah, the daughter of Adaiah of Boscath.
text.NABRE: Josiah was eight years old when he became king, and he reigned thirty-one years in Jerusalem. His mother’s name was Jedidah, daughter of Adaiah, from Bozkath.
text.DRB: Eight years old was Josias when he began to reign, he reigned one & thirty years in Jerusalem: the name of his mother was Idida, the daughter of Hadaia of Besecath.

text.NRSV: He did what was right in the sight of the Lord, and walked in all the way of his ancestor David; he did not turn aside to the right or to the left.
text.Rotherham: And he did that which was right in the eyes of Yahweh,—and walked in all the way of David his father, and turned not aside, to the right hand or to the left.
text.KJV: And he did that which was right in the sight of the Lord, and walked in all the way of David his father, and turned not aside to the right hand or to the left.
text.NABRE: He did what was right in the Lord ’s sight, walking in the way of David his father, not turning right or left.
text.DRB: And he did that which was liked before our Lord, and walked in all the ways of David his father: he declined not to the right hand, or to the left.

text.NRSV: In the eighteenth year of King Josiah, the king sent Shaphan son of Azaliah, son of Meshullam, the secretary, to the house of the Lord, saying,
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, in the eighteenth year of King Josiah, that the king sent Shaphan son of Azaliah son of Meshullam, the scribe, to the house of Yahweh, saying—
text.KJV: And it came to pass in the eighteenth year of king Josiah, that the king sent Shaphan the son of Azaliah, the son of Meshullam, the scribe, to the house of the Lord, saying,
text.NABRE: The Book of the Law. In his eighteenth year, King Josiah sent the scribe Shaphan, son of Azaliah, son of Meshullam, to the house of the Lord with these orders:
text.DRB: And in the eighteenth year of king Josias, the king sent Saphan the son of Aslia, the son of Messulam, the scribe of the temple of our Lord, saying to him:

text.NRSV: “Go up to the high priest Hilkiah, and have him count the entire sum of the money that has been brought into the house of the Lord, which the keepers of the threshold have collected from the people;
text.Rotherham: Go up unto Hilkiah, the high priest, that he pour out the silver that hath been brought into the house of Yahweh,—which the keepers of the entrance-hall have gathered from the people,
text.KJV: Go up to Hilkiah the high priest, that he may sum the silver which is brought into the house of the Lord, which the keepers of the door have gathered of the people:
text.NABRE: “Go to the high priest Hilkiah and have him calculate the valuables that have been brought to the house of the Lord, which the doorkeepers have collected from the people.
text.DRB: Go to Helcias the high Priest, that the money may be gathered into a sum, which hath been brought into the temple of our Lord, which the porters of the temple have gathered of the people,

text.NRSV: let it be given into the hand of the workers who have the oversight of the house of the Lord; let them give it to the workers who are at the house of the Lord, repairing the house,
text.Rotherham: that they may give it into the hand of the doers of the work, who have oversight of the house of Yahweh, that they may give it to the doers of the work, who are in the house of Yahweh, to repair the breaches of the house;
text.KJV: And let them deliver it into the hand of the doers of the work, that have the oversight of the house of the Lord: and let them give it to the doers of the work which is in the house of the Lord, to repair the breaches of the house,
text.NABRE: Then have him turn them over to the master workers in the house of the Lord, and have them give them to the ordinary workers who are in the house of the Lord to repair its breaches:
text.DRB: and let it be given to the workmen by the overseers of the house of our Lord, who also shall distribute it to them that work in the temple of our Lord, to make the reparations of the temple:

text.NRSV: that is, to the carpenters, to the builders, to the masons; and let them use it to buy timber and quarried stone to repair the house.
text.Rotherham: to the carpenters, and to the builders, and to the masons,—and to buy timber, and hewn stones, for repairing the house.
text.KJV: Unto carpenters, and builders, and masons, and to buy timber and hewn stone to repair the house.
text.NABRE: to the carpenters, the builders, and the masons, and to purchase wood and hewn stone.
text.DRB: that is, to the carpenters and masons, and to them that mend broken places: and that timber may be bought, and stones out of the quarries, to repair the temple of our Lord.

text.NRSV: But no accounting shall be asked from them for the money that is delivered into their hand, for they deal honestly.”
text.Rotherham: Howbeit there used to be no reckoning made with them, as to the silver that was given into their hand,—because, with faithfulness, were they dealing.
text.KJV: Howbeit there was no reckoning made with them of the money that was delivered into their hand, because they dealt faithfully.
text.NABRE: No reckoning shall be asked of them regarding the funds provided to them, because they hold positions of trust.”
text.DRB: Yet let not the money which they receive be accounted to them, but let them have it in their power, and upon their fidelity.

text.NRSV: The high priest Hilkiah said to Shaphan the secretary, “I have found the book of the law in the house of the Lord.” When Hilkiah gave the book to Shaphan, he read it.
text.Rotherham: Then said Hilkiah the high priest, unto Shaphan the scribe—The book of the law, have I found, in the house of Yahweh. So Hilkiah delivered the book unto Shaphan, and he read it.
text.KJV: And Hilkiah the high priest said unto Shaphan the scribe, I have found the book of the law in the house of the Lord. And Hilkiah gave the book to Shaphan, and he read it.
text.NABRE: The high priest Hilkiah informed the scribe Shaphan, “I have found the book of the law in the temple of the Lord.” Hilkiah gave the book to Shaphan, who read it.
text.DRB: And Helcias the high Priest said to Saphan the scribe: I have found the Book of the law in the house of our Lord: and Helcias gave the volume to Saphan, who also did read it.

text.NRSV: Then Shaphan the secretary came to the king, and reported to the king, “Your servants have emptied out the money that was found in the house, and have delivered it into the hand of the workers who have the oversight of the house of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Then came Shaphan the scribe unto the king, and brought the king word again,—and said—Thy servants have poured out the silver that was found in the house, and have delivered it unto the hand of the doers of the work, who have oversight of the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Shaphan the scribe came to the king, and brought the king word again, and said, Thy servants have gathered the money that was found in the house, and have delivered it into the hand of them that do the work, that have the oversight of the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then the scribe Shaphan went to the king and reported, “Your servants have smelted down the silver deposited in the temple and have turned it over to the master workers in the house of the Lord.”
text.DRB: Saphan also the scribe came to the king, and reported unto him that which he had commanded, and said: Thy servants have gathered into a sum the money, which is found in the house of our Lord: and they have given it to be distributed to the workmen, by the overseers of the works of the temple of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Shaphan the secretary informed the king, “The priest Hilkiah has given me a book.” Shaphan then read it aloud to the king.
text.Rotherham: Then Shaphan the scribe told the king, saying—A book, hath Hilkiah the priest delivered unto me. And Shaphan read it before the king.
text.KJV: And Shaphan the scribe shewed the king, saying, Hilkiah the priest hath delivered me a book. And Shaphan read it before the king.
text.NABRE: The scribe Shaphan also informed the king, “Hilkiah the priest has given me a book,” and then Shaphan read it in the presence of the king.
text.DRB: Saphan also the scribe told the king, saying: Helcias the priest hath given me a Book. Which when Saphan had read before the king,

text.NRSV: When the king heard the words of the book of the law, he tore his clothes.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when the king heard the words of the book of the law, that he rent his clothes.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when the king had heard the words of the book of the law, that he rent his clothes.
text.NABRE: When the king heard the words of the book of the law, he tore his garments.
text.DRB: and the king had heard the words of the law of our Lord, he rent his garments.

text.NRSV: Then the king commanded the priest Hilkiah, Ahikam son of Shaphan, Achbor son of Micaiah, Shaphan the secretary, and the king’s servant Asaiah, saying,
text.Rotherham: And the king commanded Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam son of Shaphan, and Achbor son of Micaiah, and Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the king’s servant, saying:
text.KJV: And the king commanded Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam the son of Shaphan, and Achbor the son of Michaiah, and Shaphan the scribe, and Asahiah a servant of the king’s, saying,
text.NABRE: The king then issued this command to Hilkiah the priest, Ahikam, son of Shaphan, Achbor, son of Micaiah, Shaphan the scribe, and Asaiah the king’s servant:
text.DRB: And he willed Helcias the Priest, and Ahicam the son of Saphan, and Achobor the son of Micha, and Saphan the Scribe, and Asaia the king's servant, saying:

text.NRSV: “Go, inquire of the Lord for me, for the people, and for all Judah, concerning the words of this book that has been found; for great is the wrath of the Lord that is kindled against us, because our ancestors did not obey the words of this book, to do according to all that is written concerning us.”
text.Rotherham: Go ye—enquire of Yahweh, for me and for the people, and for all Judah, concerning the words of this book which hath been found,—for, great, is the wrath of Yahweh, in that it hath fired up against us, because our fathers have not hearkened unto the words of this book, to do according to all which is written concerning us.
text.KJV: Go ye, enquire of the Lord for me, and for the people, and for all Judah, concerning the words of this book that is found: for great is the wrath of the Lord that is kindled against us, because our fathers have not hearkened unto the words of this book, to do according unto all that which is written concerning us.
text.NABRE: “Go, consult the Lord for me, for the people, and for all Judah, about the words of this book that has been found, for the rage of the Lord has been set furiously ablaze against us, because our ancestors did not obey the words of this book, nor do what is written for us.”
text.DRB: Go and consult our Lord for me, and for the people, and for all Juda, concerning the words of this volume, which is found: for the great wrath of our Lord is kindled against us: because our fathers have not heard the words of this Book, to do all that is written for us.

text.NRSV: So the priest Hilkiah, Ahikam, Achbor, Shaphan, and Asaiah went to the prophetess Huldah the wife of Shallum son of Tikvah, son of Harhas, keeper of the wardrobe (she lived in Jerusalem in the Second Quarter), and spoke with her.
text.Rotherham: So Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam, and Achbor, and Shaphan, and Asaiah, went unto Huldah the prophetess, wife of Shallum son of Tikvah, son of Harhas keeper of the vestments, she having her dwelling in Jerusalem, in the new city,—and they spake unto her.
text.KJV: So Hilkiah the priest, and Ahikam, and Achbor, and Shaphan, and Asahiah, went unto Huldah the prophetess, the wife of Shallum the son of Tikvah, the son of Harhas, keeper of the wardrobe; (now she dwelt in Jerusalem in the college;) and they communed with her.
text.NABRE: So Hilkiah the priest, Ahikam, Achbor, Shaphan, and Asaiah went to Huldah the prophet, wife of Shallum, son of Tikvah, son of Harhas, keeper of the wardrobe; she lived in Jerusalem, in the Second Quarter. When they had spoken to her,
text.DRB: Helcias therefore the Priest, and Ahicam, and Achobor, and Saphan, and Asaia went to Holda a prophetess, the wife of Sellum, the son of Thecua, the son of Araas keeper of the garments, who dwelt in Jerusalem in the second: and they spake to her.

text.NRSV: She said to them, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Tell the man who sent you to me,
text.Rotherham: And she said unto them, Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, Say ye unto the man who hath sent you unto me:
text.KJV: And she said unto them, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Tell the man that sent you to me,
text.NABRE: she said to them, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Say to the man who sent you to me,
text.DRB: And she answered them: Thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: Tell the man, that sent you to me:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord, I will indeed bring disaster on this place and on its inhabitants—all the words of the book that the king of Judah has read.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh, Behold me! about to bring calamity upon this place, and upon the inhabitants thereof,—all the words of the book, which, the king of Judah, hath read:
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord, Behold, I will bring evil upon this place, and upon the inhabitants thereof, even all the words of the book which the king of Judah hath read:
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord: I am about to bring evil upon this place and upon its inhabitants—all the words of the book which the king of Judah has read.
text.DRB: Thus saith our Lord: Behold, I will bring evils upon this place, and upon the inhabitants thereof, all the words of the law which the king of Juda hath read:

text.NRSV: Because they have abandoned me and have made offerings to other gods, so that they have provoked me to anger with all the work of their hands, therefore my wrath will be kindled against this place, and it will not be quenched.
text.Rotherham: Because they have forsaken me, and burned incense unto other gods, that they might provoke me to anger, with all the workmanship of their hands; and so my wrath shall fire up against this place, and shall not be quenched:
text.KJV: Because they have forsaken me, and have burned incense unto other gods, that they might provoke me to anger with all the works of their hands; therefore my wrath shall be kindled against this place, and shall not be quenched.
text.NABRE: Because they have abandoned me and have burned incense to other gods, provoking me by all the works of their hands, my rage is ablaze against this place and it cannot be extinguished.
text.DRB: because they have forsaken me, and have sacrificed to strange gods, provoking me in all the works of their hands: and my indignation shall be kindled in this place, and shall not be quenched.

text.NRSV: But as to the king of Judah, who sent you to inquire of the Lord, thus shall you say to him, Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: Regarding the words that you have heard,
text.Rotherham: But, unto the king of Judah, who hath sent you to enquire of Yahweh, thus, shall ye say unto him,—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel, As touching the words which thou hast heard:
text.KJV: But to the king of Judah which sent you to enquire of the Lord, thus shall ye say to him, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, As touching the words which thou hast heard;
text.NABRE: “But to the king of Judah who sent you to consult the Lord, give this response: Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: As for the words you have heard,
text.DRB: But to the king of Juda, that sent you to consult our Lord, thus you shall say: Thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: For that thou hast heard the words of the volume,

text.NRSV: because your heart was penitent, and you humbled yourself before the Lord, when you heard how I spoke against this place, and against its inhabitants, that they should become a desolation and a curse, and because you have torn your clothes and wept before me, I also have heard you, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Because, tender, was thy heart, and thou didst humble thyself before Yahweh when thou heardest what I had spoken against this place and against the inhabitants thereof—that they should become a desolation and a curse, and didst rend thy clothes, and weep before me, therefore also, I, have hearkened—declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: Because thine heart was tender, and thou hast humbled thyself before the Lord, when thou heardest what I spake against this place, and against the inhabitants thereof, that they should become a desolation and a curse, and hast rent thy clothes, and wept before me; I also have heard thee, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: because you were heartsick and have humbled yourself before the Lord when you heard what I have spoken concerning this place and its inhabitants, that they would become a desolation and a curse; and because you tore your garments and wept before me, I in turn have heard, oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: and thy heart is strucken with fear, and thou art humbled before the Lord, hearing the words against this place, and the inhabitants thereof, to wit, that they should become a wonder and a curse: and hast rent thy garments, and wept before me, and I have heard it, saith our Lord:

text.NRSV: Therefore, I will gather you to your ancestors, and you shall be gathered to your grave in peace; your eyes shall not see all the disaster that I will bring on this place.” They took the message back to the king.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, behold me! about to gather thee unto thy fathers, and thou shalt be gathered unto thy graves, in peace, and so thine eyes shall not look upon all the misfortune which I am about to bring upon this place. And they brought the king word again.
text.KJV: Behold therefore, I will gather thee unto thy fathers, and thou shalt be gathered into thy grave in peace; and thine eyes shall not see all the evil which I will bring upon this place. And they brought the king word again.
text.NABRE: I will therefore gather you to your ancestors; you shall go to your grave in peace, and your eyes shall not see all the evil I am about to bring upon this place.” This they reported to the king.
text.DRB: therefore I will gather thee to thy fathers, and thou shalt be gathered to thy sepulchre in peace, that thy eyes may not see all the evils, which I will bring in upon this place.

text.NRSV: Then the king directed that all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem should be gathered to him.
text.Rotherham: Then the king sent,—and there were gathered unto him, all the elders of Judah, and Jerusalem;
text.KJV: And the king sent, and they gathered unto him all the elders of Judah and of Jerusalem.
text.NABRE: The king then had all the elders of Judah and of Jerusalem summoned before him.
text.DRB: And they reported to the king that which she had said. Who sent: and all the ancients of Juda and Jerusalem were assembled to him.

text.NRSV: The king went up to the house of the Lord, and with him went all the people of Judah, all the inhabitants of Jerusalem, the priests, the prophets, and all the people, both small and great; he read in their hearing all the words of the book of the covenant that had been found in the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and the king went up to the house of Yahweh, and all the men of Judah and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem with him, and the priests, and the prophets, and all the people, both small and great,—and he read in their ears, all the words of the book of the covenant, which had been found in the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the king went up into the house of the Lord, and all the men of Judah and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem with him, and the priests, and the prophets, and all the people, both small and great: and he read in their ears all the words of the book of the covenant which was found in the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The king went up to the house of the Lord with all the people of Judah and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem: priests, prophets, and all the people, great and small. He read aloud to them all the words of the book of the covenant that had been found in the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: And the king went up to the temple of our Lord, & all the men of Juda, and all that dwelt in Jerusalem with him Priests and Prophets, and all the people from little to great: and in hearing of all he read all the words of the Book of the covenant, which was found in the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: The king stood by the pillar and made a covenant before the Lord, to follow the Lord, keeping his commandments, his decrees, and his statutes, with all his heart and all his soul, to perform the words of this covenant that were written in this book. All the people joined in the covenant.
text.Rotherham: And the king took his stand by the pillar, and solemnised a covenant before Yahweh—to follow Yahweh, and to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his statutes, with all [their] heart and with all [their] soul, to confirm the words of this covenant, written in this book. And all the people took their stand in the covenant.
text.KJV: And the king stood by a pillar, and made a covenant before the Lord, to walk after the Lord, and to keep his commandments and his testimonies and his statutes with all their heart and all their soul, to perform the words of this covenant that were written in this book. And all the people stood to the covenant.
text.NABRE: The king stood by the column and made a covenant in the presence of the Lord to follow the Lord and to observe his commandments, statutes, and decrees with his whole heart and soul, and to re-establish the words of the covenant written in this book. And all the people stood by the covenant.
text.DRB: And the king stood upon the step: and made a covenant with our Lord, to walk after our Lord, and keep his precepts, and testimonies, and ceremonies, with all their heart, and with all their soul, and to perform the words of this covenant, which were written in that Book: and the people agreed to the covenant:

text.NRSV: The king commanded the high priest Hilkiah, the priests of the second order, and the keepers of the threshold, to bring out of the temple of the Lord all the vessels made for Baal, for Asherah, and for all the host of heaven; he burned them outside Jerusalem in the fields of the Kidron, and carried their ashes to Bethel.
text.Rotherham: Then the king commanded Hilkiah the high priest, and the priests of the second order, and the keepers of the entrance-hall, to bring forth, out of the temple of Yahweh, all the vessels that had been made for Baal and for the Sacred Stem, and for all the army of the heavens,—and he burned them up outside Jerusalem, in the fields of Kidron, and carried the ashes of them to Bethel;
text.KJV: And the king commanded Hilkiah the high priest, and the priests of the second order, and the keepers of the door, to bring forth out of the temple of the Lord all the vessels that were made for Baal, and for the grove, and for all the host of heaven: and he burned them without Jerusalem in the fields of Kidron, and carried the ashes of them unto Beth–el.
text.NABRE: Josiah’s Religious Reform. Then the king commanded the high priest Hilkiah, his assistant priests, and the doorkeepers to remove from the temple of the Lord all the objects that had been made for Baal, Asherah, and the whole host of heaven. These he burned outside Jerusalem on the slopes of the Kidron; their ashes were carried to Bethel.
text.DRB: And the king willed Helcias the high priest, and the priests of the second order, and all the porters that they should cast out of the temple of our Lord all the vessels that had been made to Baal, and in the grove, and to all the host of heaven: & he burnt them without Jerusalem in the Valley cedron, and carried the dust of them into Bethel.

text.NRSV: He deposed the idolatrous priests whom the kings of Judah had ordained to make offerings in the high places at the towns of Judah and around Jerusalem; those also who made offerings to Baal, to the sun, the moon, the constellations, and all the host of the heavens.
text.Rotherham: and he put down the idol-priests, whom the kings of Judah had appointed, so that incense might be burned in the high places, in the cities of Judah, and round about Jerusalem,—them also that burned incense to Baal, to the sun, and to the moon, and to the constellations, and to all the army of the heavens;
text.KJV: And he put down the idolatrous priests, whom the kings of Judah had ordained to burn incense in the high places in the cities of Judah, and in the places round about Jerusalem; them also that burned incense unto Baal, to the sun, and to the moon, and to the planets, and to all the host of heaven.
text.NABRE: He also put an end to the idolatrous priests whom the kings of Judah had appointed to burn incense on the high places in the cities of Judah and in the vicinity of Jerusalem, as well as those who burned incense to Baal, to the sun, moon, and signs of the zodiac, and to the whole host of heaven.
text.DRB: And he destroyed the Soothsayers, which the kings of Juda had appointed to sacrifice in the excelses in the cities of Juda, and round about Jerusalem: and them that burnt incense to Baal, and to the Sun, and to the Moon, and to the twelve signs, and to all the host of heaven.

text.NRSV: He brought out the image of Asherah from the house of the Lord, outside Jerusalem, to the wadi Kidron, burned it at the wadi Kidron, beat it to dust and threw the dust of it upon the graves of the common people.
text.Rotherham: and he brought forth the Sacred Stem out of the house of Yahweh, outside Jerusalem, into the Kidron ravine, and burned it in the Kidron ravine, and crushed it to powder,—and cast the powder upon the graves of the sons of the people;
text.KJV: And he brought out the grove from the house of the Lord, without Jerusalem, unto the brook Kidron, and burned it at the brook Kidron, and stamped it small to powder, and cast the powder thereof upon the graves of the children of the people.
text.NABRE: From the house of the Lord he also removed the Asherah to the Wadi Kidron, outside Jerusalem; he burned it and beat it to dust, in the Wadi Kidron, and scattered its dust over the graveyard of the people of the land.
text.DRB: And he caused the grove to be carried forth out of the house of our Lord without Jerusalem in the Valley cedron, and he burnt it there, and brought it into dust, and threw it upon the sepulchres of the common people.

text.NRSV: He broke down the houses of the male temple prostitutes that were in the house of the Lord, where the women wove hangings for the Asherah.
text.Rotherham: and he brake down the houses of the male devotees, which were in the house of Yahweh,—where the women did weave houses to the Sacred Stem;
